Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Atomic subsystem with constant and discrete sample time blocks connected to Switch block may produce wrong answers
Description: An atomic subsystem connected to a Switch block may produce wrong answers under the following conditions: The subsystem contains both constant sample time and discrete sample time blocks The subsystem's output is connected to a data input port of a Switch block The model configuration parameter? 'Conditional input branch execution' ?is checked
Workaround: Uncheck 'Conditional input branch execution'?in the model configuration parameters?or?Change the sample times of any Constant blocks inside the atomic subsystem to discrete (i.e., change from "inf" to "-1")

Summary: Simulink crashes on macOS Mojave 10.14.4 when opening model or using viewmarks
Description: Simulink might crash when opening models or using viewmarks on macOS Mojave 10.14.4. If you encounter this crash, the stack trace includes this line: +[QTMovie_QuickTime movieFileTypes:]
Workaround: Installation instructions  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report. If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.  Extract the files from the .zip file into a folder on the MATLAB path. For a single-user installation, you can place the contents in ~/Documents/MATLAB.  Delete the .zip file.Add the command?fix_ebr_1995828; to your startup.m file, before calling any Simulink-related commands in that file.Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Chart property?Initialize Outputs Every Time Chart Wakes Up is not honored when using MATLAB as the Action Language
Description: In a chart that uses MATLAB as the action language, the chart property?Initialize Outputs Every Time Chart Wakes Up ?is not honored. The chart does not reset outputs implicitly at each new time step. Chart output values persist until they are explicitly overwritten. This behavior leads to incorrect results in both simulation and the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect variable reuse when a Unit Delay block connects to a Constant block inside an enabled, triggered, or function-call subsystem
Description: When a Unit Delay block is inside a triggered, function-call, or enabled subsystem, the state variable of the Unit Delay block?may be incorrectly reused?if all of the following conditions are true: O ptimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or?Minimize global data access . R euse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled. The Unit Delay input signal also connects to a subsystem outport block, which has null initial conditions. The Unit Delay input signal is driven by a Constant or Initial Condition block. The initial conditions of the Unit Delay and driving block do not match.
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727. That report describes a different but related issue, and the same workaround applies.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable?custom storage class?specification at the input of a Unit Delay block can lead to incorrect code
Description: In code, the state of a Unit Delay block may be incorrectly reused with a reusable custom storage class variable if all of the following are true: Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results or Minimize global data access   . Reuse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled. The input to?a Unit Delay block has a reusable?custom storage class?specification. That input signal is also connected to a root output port. The?same reusable?custom storage class?specification is at the root input port.  For the attached example, the incorrect code generation pattern is as follows: ?abc = abc + abc;  Here, abc  is a variable corresponding to reusable custom storage class signal specification. In this example, the state variable got incorrectly merged into abc .
Workaround: To workaround around the issue, do either of the following:See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727. That report describes a different but related issue, and the same workaround applies.Remove the?reusable custom storage class?specification from the input of the Unit Delay block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when the sample times of the input and output of a Unit Delay block are different and?Optimize global data access?parameter is set to a value other than?none
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a Unit Delay block if all of the following conditions are true: The sample time of the Unit Delay's input is different from the sample time of the destination block of the Unit Delay's output. Optimize global data access  configuration parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results  or Minimize global data access.  Reuse local block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled.  When these conditions are met, the generated code might?contain the wrong value for the?Unit Delay block's?output.??
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727. That report describes a different but related issue, and the same workaround applies.?

Summary: Duplicate declarations of functions for subsystems
Description: When a model contains a subsystem that has a single, asynchronous sample time, and the subsystem is configured to generate a reusable or nonreusable function, the code generated for the model may contain duplicate, identical declarations of those functions. For example,?? extern void SS2(real_T rtu_In1, real_T *rty_Out1);  extern void SS2(real_T rtu_In1, real_T *rty_Out1);  ?

Summary: Selecting?a MAT file containing duplicate variables in the current folder browser causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When a?MAT file is?selected in the current folder browser, MATLAB loads the MAT file and displays variable details in the detail panel. If the file?contains duplicate variables, MATLAB crashes while trying to load the variables.? Note:  MATLAB does not allow duplicate variables?when creating MAT files. However, some third party tools used?to create MAT files do allow duplicate?variables, therefore causing the crash.?

Summary: Using Unicode string in Stateflow and MATLAB Function blocks can crash MATLAB during model simulation
Description: Using a Unicode string in MATLAB code inside of a Stateflow or MATLAB Function block can crash MATLAB during simulation. The affected blocks are MATLAB Function block, Truth Table, MATLAB Action Language Chart, State Transition Table, and Test Sequence blocks. Writing Unicode strings in comments is safe. ? ?

Summary: Spurious error when generating code for a model containing a Simulink Function called only from a Stateflow chart
Description: When generating code for a model containing a Simulink Function, and the Simulink Function is called only from a Stateflow chart, Simulink incorrectly reports an error that this Simulink Function is not called in this model:  Simulink Coder does not support models containing Simulink Functions that are not called. The model contains the uncalled Simulink Function 'myFunction' defined by block 'myModel/myFunction'.
Workaround: Create a Function Caller block that is configured to call this Simulink Function in addition to the Stateflow chart, or change the function into a graphical, truth table, or MATLAB function inside the chart. If you choose the latter, and want to call this function from other charts as well, then configure the chart to export functions for reuse.

Summary: Build error "identifier has already been reserved" for AUTOSAR model when using data type replacement
Description: In AUTOSAR models that use data type replacement, if a replacement name specified for a data type matches an AUTOSAR implementation data type name, code generation might fail with the error "The identifier 'typename ' has already been reserved ". For example, suppose that you import an AUTOSAR implementation data type named float32  from arxml  files. If you then configure data type replacement for the model, and specify float32  as a replacement name for 32-bit floating point data, code generation might fail with the following error: The identifier 'float32' has already been reserved

Description: If you have accessibility tools running on your computer, for example, AccEvent.exe from the Microsoft SDK, closing a Simulink window or dialog box can cause MATLAB to crash. The stack trace generated will contain references to QAccessible::queryAccessibleInterface , for example: [ 0] 0x000000008bd9e3a4 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2014b\bin\win64\QtCore4.dll+01106852 QObject::metaObject+00000004 [ 1] 0x00000000bf132072 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2014b\bin\win64\QtGui4.dll+04464754 QAccessible::queryAccessibleInterface+00000050 [ 2] 0x00000000bf136f86 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2014b\bin\win64\QtGui4.dll+04484998 QAccessiblePlugin::~QAccessiblePlugin+00000342 [ 3] 0x00000000bf139147 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2014b\bin\win64\QtGui4.dll+04493639 QAccessiblePlugin::~QAccessiblePlugin+00008983 [ 4] 0x00007ffee6b02904 C:\Windows\System32\OLEACC.dll+00010500 DllCanUnloadNow+00006388 [ 5] 0x00007ffedf325b82 C:\Windows\System32\uiautomationcore.dll+00154498 UiaDisconnectProvider+00095202

Summary: Modifying size of for-loop index variable can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you change the size of the for-loop index variable from inside the body of the loop, MATLAB can crash. For example: i = 1;for theta = 0:10:90    theta(i) = theta;    i=i+1;end

Summary: Loading DOM objects from a MAT file crashes MATLAB
Description: Loading DOM objects from a MAT file crashes MATLAB. For example, this script crashes MATLAB: import mlreportgen.dom.*t = Text('Hello World');p = Paragraph(t);save('dom','p','t');clear('p');load('dom');

Summary: Incorrect value is displayed when Value Label is enabled on a signal
Description: If all the blocks in a model or subsystem have a sample time of inf , then enabling Value Labels  on a signal may show the incorrect value. You would typically see the same values repeated for all signals in the model.
Workaround: In the model configuration parameters, go into All Parameters tab, uncheck Optimization > Reuse local block outputs. It is also recommended to uncheck Optimization > Signal storage reuse. Note that these changes may lead to an increase in memory usage. You can also use Display blocks instead of value labels to view the signal values.

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation may fail for a model that contains a Constant block that outputs a nonvirtual bus signal to a root outport block
Description: Simulating a model in Accelerator  mode may fail if the model that meets the following conditions: A Constant block outputs a nonvirtual bus signal. The nonvirtual bus signal feeds into a Bus Selector block. At least one of the signals from the Bus Selector block feeds into a root outport block
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can do either of the following:1. On the Optimization>Signals and Parameters pane, deselect the Inline invariant signals parameter or enter the following command in the MATLAB Command Window:set_param(bdroot, 'InlineInvariantSignals, 0)This change causes a slight increase in code size.2. Replace the Constant block that outputs a nonvirtual bus signal with individual constant blocks that output constant scalars into a Bus Creator block.

Summary: Custom icon for deployed standalone applications does not appear in Windows XP
Description: When a user adds their own custom icon using deploytool  for a standalone application and deploys the application on any Windows XP machine, the custom icon does not appear. A default Windows executable icon shows instead. Starting with R2015b, MATLAB and Compiler generated deployed components no longer support Windows XP.
Workaround: Use a third party resource editor tool, such as Resource Hacker, to change the icon for XP after the executable is created, and then distribute the modified executable.

Summary: Cannot discover clusters on Amazon EC2
Description: In R2015aSP1, the Discover Clusters dialog box fails to display clusters running on Amazon EC2. Similarly, fetchCloudClusters  fails with the message: Error using parallel.cluster.MJSComputeCloud.hDiscoverClusters (line 35)Java exception occurred:java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException: Unmatched closing ')' near index 6^R2015a) Service Pack 1.*  You cannot create or set profiles for cloud clusters without downloading a profile first. You should only see this bug in service pack releases (R2015aSP1).
Workaround: To create and set profiles for clusters running on Amazon EC2, follow these steps:1) Go to the MathWorks Cloud Center at https://cloudcenter.mathworks.com 2) Navigate to "My Clusters" and click on the name of the desired cluster.3) Download the cluster profile to your computer using the MATLAB Cluster Profile download button.4) In MATLAB, select Parallel > Manage Cluster Profiles, and import the downloaded profile. You can set the profile to the default one within the same Cluster Profile Manager.?

Summary: Stateflow does not display text exceeding 35 lines in edit mode
Description: If the text content of a Stateflow graphical object (states, transitions, etc.) exceeds 35 lines, the text beyond 35 lines is not displayed when you first enter edit mode. After you type a character or change the zoom level, the text reappears.

Summary: Rapid accelerator code regeneration due to mask variable used in tunable block parameter
Description: In general, when you simulate a model in rapid accelerator mode, the model does not regenerate code after you change the value of a tunable block parameter or workspace variable. However, the model unexpectedly regenerates code if all of these conditions are true: You use mask initialization code to create a numeric variable, for example myMaskVar . You use the variable to set the value of a block parameter such as the Gain  parameter of a Gain block. You create a numeric variable or Simulink.Parameter  object, for example myWkspVar , in a workspace or data dictionary. You use the variable to set the value of the same block parameter that uses the mask variable. For example, you set the value of the block parameter to myMaskVar + myWkspVar . You also use the workspace variable to set the value of a different tunable block parameter. You use the function Simulink.BlockDiagram.buildRapidAcceleratorTarget  to generate a structure of tunable parameters from the model. The workspace variable appears in the structure, so you can tune the variable value. You tune the value of the workspace variable by using the tunable parameter structure. When you simulate, the model regenerates code.  ? ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation may occur when a Constant block feeds directly into a To File block and the value of the Constant block is an expression containing a variable
Description: The code generator may create a non-pointer variable to hold the input value of a To File block and then incorrectly dereference this variable if the following conditions exist: A Constant block feeds directly into a To File block. The value of the Constant block is an expression containing a variable.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block on the path between the output of Constant block and the input of the To File block. On the Signal Conversion block, select the parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Copying and pasting commented blocks causes segmentation violation
Description: A segmentation violation can occur when you copy and paste parts of your model that contain commented-out or commented-through blocks.
Workaround: If parts of your model contain commented-out or commented-through blocks, uncomment those blocks before a copy-paste operation.

Summary: Design Error Detection using Simulink Design Verifier returns all objectives as 'Undecided' for models with arrays of more than 200 constant integer or fixed-point elements
Description: Running Design Error Detection analysis using Simulink Design Verifier produces no results for models with arrays of more than 200 constant integer or fixed-point elements. The analysis finishes without showing any errors, but the status of all objectives will be 'Undecided'. Examples where such arrays can originate include block parameters and MATLAB variables.
Workaround: Except 'Integer overflow' check, the remaining Design Error Detection checks can be run after setting the Data type override parameter of the model to double.

Summary: Large data items in referenced model can disproportionately increase memory usage
Description: A referenced model can include data, for example, parameter data and From Workspace block data. If the referenced model includes a large amount of data, say, greater than 25MB, the memory used by the model can increase disproportionately. When you simulate your model in the Accelerator mode or generate code for the model, the model data is embedded in a library file. For example:  slprj/sim/modellib.a  For each 25 MB increase in model data, the size of the modellib.a  file increases by 25 MB. Depending on the depth of the model reference hierarchy, a checksum calculation on the modellib.a  file may be performed. For each 25 MB increase in data, the memory used in the checksum calculation is 8 x 25 MB, i.e., 200 MB. The large increase in memory usage can adversely affect performance, during simulation or code generation.

Summary: Commands bdroot and gcb return incorrect paths after copying and pasting blocks that have test harnesses.
Description: If you copy a block that has a test harness, and paste it in the model canvas, bdroot  returns a path that incorrectly points to one of the pasted test harnesses instead of returning the name of the current top-level system.  The command gcb  incorrectly returns the handle of a block in the test harness.

Summary: Failure to start parallel pools or jobs on Windows
Description: Parallel pools or other jobs using the local scheduler can fail on some Windows machines with the message: "Microsoft HPC MPI Application Launch has stopped working."

Summary: Code Generation errors if a tunable structure parameter contains non-finite fields.
Description: If a model uses a tunable structure parameter which contains non-finite fields, an error is issued in code generation. It is a known limitation that Embedded Coder currently does not support initialization of non-finite fields of structures.
Workaround: If you reference the non-finite structure field, you can add an arbitrary string field to the structure, which will cause the generated code to contain the non-finite value instead of the structure as a whole.

Summary: Opening a test harness for a pasted block results in an unexpected assertion
Description: If you perform either of the following two sequences of operations, then try to open a test harness for the pasted block, Simulink returns an unexpected assertion, "Invalid Simulink Object Handle." Select a block with test harnesses, then copy, delete, and paste the block in the Simulink editor. Select a block with test harnesses and then copy and paste the block.  Then, delete the original block and paste again.

Summary: Performing cut, undo, and paste operations on a block with test harnesses results in a pasted block without test harnesses.
Description: If you select a block and the block has one or more test harnesses, then you perform the following sequence of operations on the block: Cut Undo Paste   The pasted block does not contain the test harness(es).

Summary: "libGL error: unable to load driver" during MATLAB? startup
Description: During MATLAB startup, you might see output like this in the Linux console: libGL error: dlopen /usr/lib64/dri/nouveau_dri.so failed (/usr/local/MATLAB/R2014b/bin/glnxa64/../../sys/os/glnxa64/libstdc++.so.6: version `GLIBCXX_3.4.18' not found (required by /lib64/libLLVM-3.5-mesa.so))libGL error: unable to load driver: nouveau_dri.solibGL error: driver pointer missinglibGL error: failed to load driver: nouveaulibGL error: dlopen /usr/lib64/dri/swrast_dri.so failed (/usr/local/MATLAB/R2014b/bin/glnxa64/../../sys/os/glnxa64/libstdc++.so.6: version `GLIBCXX_3.4.18' not found (required by /lib64/libLLVM-3.5-mesa.so))libGL error: unable to load driver: swrast_dri.solibGL error: failed to load driver: swrast This output indicates that MATLAB is unable to load the graphics driver and will use software OpenGL? instead.
Workaround: These errors indicate that MATLAB is using software OpenGL? instead of the graphics driver. Thus, you can safely ignore the error messages.

Summary: Incorrect range displayed after a pointer conversion
Description: If you run verification on the following code, a red User assertion  check incorrectly appears on the assert()  function after the pointer conversion. The range of *charPtr  is the full range allowed for a char  variable. Following the conversion, the range of *shortPtr  must be the full range allowed for a short  variable. Because this range includes values both greater and less than 500, the User assertion  check on the assert()  function must be orange. On the Source  pane, if you place your cursor on *  in the assert  statement, the tooltip incorrectly displays that the range of  *shortPtr  is the full range for a char  variable.   #include <assert.h> #define SIZE 10 extern char random();  void func(void) { char arr[SIZE]; /* Array members initialized to full-range value*/ for (int i=0; i <SIZE; i++)    arr[i]=random(); /* Pointer assignment and conversion */    char *charPtr = arr;    short *shortPtr = (short *)charPtr ;    assert(*shortPtr > 500); // Incorrect red check}

Summary: Range analysis using Fixed-Point Designer may produce no results for models with large arrays
Description: Running range analysis using Fixed-Point Designer may produce no results for models with arrays of more than 200 constant integer or fixed-point elements. The analysis finishes without showing any errors, but the range results are empty. Examples where such arrays can originate include block parameters and MATLAB variables.
Workaround: To get results from the analysis, set the Data type override parameter of the model to double.

Description: If Xilinx Vivado 14.4 or later is your synthesis tool and your MATLAB installation path has spaces in it, such as "C:\Program Files\", you will see an error message similar to the following: ERROR: [Common 17-165] Too many positional options when parsing 'Files\MATLAB\R2015a\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcommon\+downstreamtools\extractVivadoTiming.tcl', please type 'source -help' for usage info.

To see the contents of the .zip file: Extract the files from the .zip file. Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect red  'Non-Terminating Call' check using '-inline' option
Description: In the following pseudo code, if you inline the function addNewmyStruct  using an analysis option, Polyspace incorrectly displays a red Non-Terminating Call (NTC) on the first strcpy  call. struct mystruct { char aa[16]; char bb[11]; char cc[16]; int dd; }; struct mystruct* addNewmyStruct(char* aa1, char* bb2, char* cc3, int dd4){      /* Allocate structure with malloc */     struct mystruct* nms;      nms = malloc(sizeof(struct mystruct));     /* copy function arguments to structure members */     strcpy(nms->aa,aa1); // red NTC when inlining    strcpy(nms->bb,bb2);     strcpy(nms->cc,cc3);    nms->dd = dd4;    return nms; }  Call addNewmyStruct  as follows: arraymystruct[0] = addNewmyStruct("aa", "bb", "cc", 1); arraymystruct[1] = addNewmyStruct("aa1", "bb1", "cc1", 2);  ?

Summary: Viewmark not saved with model if spaces in subsystem path
Description: Simulink reports an error when you save a model with a viewmark under certain conditions. When this error appears, Simulink saves the model but does not save the viewmark. This error occurs when: A subsystem in the model has a space in its hierarchy path name. You create a viewmark of the subsystem. You copy the viewmark to the viewmarks gallery to save with the model.   ?
Workaround: You can work around this problem using one of the two following workarounds.Remove the space in the subsystem hierarchy path.Unzip the attached file and put viewmarker.p in (matlabroot)/toolbox/shared/dastudio/+DAStudio/@Viewmarker, where (matlabroot) is the root folder of your MATLAB installation. Execute >> rehash toolboxcache from the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Blocks with only DOORS Surrogate links highlighted
Description: When synchronizing a Simulink model with a Surrogate module in DOORS and then highlighting requirements in the model, RMI incorrectly highlights all blocks that have a Surrogate link. For example, if a Simulink model was synchronized with the "Complete" option enabled, every object in the model has a Surrogate link; therefore, all blocks will be highlighted, and the user can no longer rely on RMI highlighting to locate blocks with the actual (non-Surrogate) links. ?
Workaround: Install the attached patch.Backup the getLinkedHandles.* files under the [MATLAB_INSTALL_DIR]/toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/+rmidata/ folder and unzip the contents of attached archive.

Summary: Crash when running model slicer on models with unconnected lines
Description: Running Model Slicer on a model with unconnected lines might result in a MATLAB crash with symptoms similar to the following  [  0] 0x00007faac4e2e44b ggb_commented(SLBlock const*) [  1] 0x00007faac51b4dec isCommentedThrough [  2] 0x00007faac51b4bef visitSegment [  3] 0x00007faac51b4fa8 walkThroughBlock

Summary: dbquit or Ctrl+C does not stop simulation when paused at a breakpoint
Description: When you are paused at a breakpoint in a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block, you cannot stop the simulation using dbquit  or Ctrl+C .
Workaround: Use the Stop Simulation button instead.

Summary: MATLAB Production Server command scripts not functional after abrupt stop
Description: If the MATLAB Production Server (MPS) instance, mps_master_app.exe , crashes or is killed forcefully, the server command scripts, mps-start , mps-stop  or mps-status , may not function correctly afterwards. If the commands are executed, it may hang or return the following error: Error: ping: control endpoint file exists, but main pid file missing
Workaround: Delete the pid and endpoint directories inside your MPS instance directory, if they exist, and rerun the commands.

Summary: Generated code for Direct Lookup Table block may contain C math floor() function instead of C implicit cast
Description: For an index expression that must be positive, the Direct Lookup Table block requires a float to integer type conversion. The generated code may contain a C math floor()  function to handle this type conversion even though a C implicit cast produces the same result as floor()  and is less expensive.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Generated code does not use initial value for signal when you select Ignore test point signals
Description: The code that you generate from a model does not use the initial value that you specify for a signal if all of these conditions are true: You select Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Ignore test point signals . This configuration parameter appears only if you use an ERT-based code generation target such as ert.tlc . You use a Simulink.Signal  or mpt.Signal object to represent the signal. The signal uses the storage class SimulinkGlobal . You use the object property InitialValue  to specify the initial value.
Workaround: Clear Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Ignore test point signals. To clear this configuration parameter at the command prompt using the function get_param, set IgnoreTestpoints to off:set_param(bdroot, 'IgnoreTestpoints', 'off')

Summary: AXI4-Stream interface IP core generates incorrect TLAST signal when packet size is bigger than 2048
Description: You can use HDL Workflow Advisor IP Core Generation workflow to generate an HDL IP core with AXI4-Stream interface. The AXI4-Stream master interface in the generated IP core uses a counter to generate TLAST signal to indicate packet size. The counter size is 11 bits, which is not big enough for any packet size larger than 2048. When the packet size is bigger than 2048, the counter size causes the AXI4-Stream master interface to generate an incorrect TLAST signal .

Summary: MATLAB Crashes if 'ctrl + c' is executed during graphics startup.
Description: If users hit 'ctrl + c' during the first MATLAB graphics command, MATLAB crashes while loading graphics libraries.Ex.% In a fresh MATLAB session% runaxes;% Immediately hit 'ctrl + c'
Workaround: Create a startup file that includes the following command:osginit;Refer to the following documentation page for more information regarding a startup file:https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/startup.html

Summary: Changing SPI ClockPhase and ClockPolarity for NI 845x devices has no effect
Description: When using the spi  interface with ni845x  supported devices (NI USB-8451 or NI USB-8452), setting ClockPhase  to SecondEdge  and/or ClockPolarity  to IdleHigh  has no effect, and the actual configuration used corresponds to the default property values, FirstEdge  and IdleLow  respectively. The following code is an example spi  configuration affected by this issue: s = spi('ni845x', 0, 0); s.ClockPhase = SecondEdge; s.ClockPolarity = IdleHigh;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect model argument tuning behavior when simulating a model hierarchy
Description: Simulation might produce unexpected results when all of these conditions apply: The model hierarchy has at least three levels. The top model contains multiple instances of a referenced model. The referenced model?contains a Model block that specifies values for model arguments. You change the value of at least one of these model arguments during simulation.  Only one instance of the referenced model receives updated model argument values.

Summary: Saved network parameter objects such as sparameters, yparameters, etc. will not load correctly.
Description: There are some RF Toolbox objects that will load without error in R2015a, but the objects will be in a corrupted state, and the data within those objects will not be accessible. The problem will only occur if one of the objects was saved into a *.mat file in an earlier release (R2014b or earlier). The affected classes are: sparametersyparameterszparametershparametersgparametersabcdparameters  To reproduce this error, you will need access to an R2015a release of MATLAB as well as an earlier release. In the earlier release (R2012b through R2014b), create and save an sparameters  object: S = sparameters('passive.s2p');save('test1263204','S')  Then, in R2015a, try to load the sparameters  object and plot it: load test1263204rfplot(S)  You will get the following error: Cell contents reference from a non-cell array object.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. Summary: Global variable input definition section missing from MATLAB? Coder? app without Fixed-Point Designer?
Description: If the Fixed-Point Designer? software is not installed, the global variable input definition section  is missing from the Define Input Types step of the MATLAB?  Coder? app.

Summary: MATLAB exits during HDL code generation for nested conditional subsystems
Description: MATLAB exits when HDL Coder generates code for a conditional subsystem (Enabled Subsystem or Triggered Subsystem) that meets all  of these conditions: The subsystem is a nested conditional subsystem. The high-level conditional subsystem has an output signal that is driven by the low-level conditional subsystem. The output signal does not have any blocks containing state (such as a Delay block) between the low-level conditional subsystem and the output port in the high-level subsystem. The output signal in the low-level conditional subsystem has a loop.
Workaround: Modify the model to avoid any one of the four conditions. For example, put a Delay block on the signal in the high-level conditional subsystem to avoid the third condition, and thus avoid the problem.

Summary: Rename All does not replace variable name in model argument list
Description: When you use a model as a referenced model, you can declare variables in the referenced model workspace as model arguments. When you use Rename All  in the Model Explorer to rename one of these variables, the operation does not replace the name of the variable in the model argument list.
Workaround: Manually replace the variable name in the model argument list.For example, in the Model Hierarchy pane of the Model Explorer, select the target model workspace. In the Dialog pane, replace the variable name in the Model arguments (for referencing this model) field.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Binary-point multiplication operations incorrectly replaced with MulShRight AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library (CRL) entry
Description: The code generator can incorrectly replace a binary-point multiplication operation with a MulShRight  AUTOSAR 4.0 CRL entry if all of the following conditions are true:Code generator is configured to use the AUTOSAR 4.0 CRL. Ideal result of the multiplication operation is signed. Multiplication operation rounds to zero.  The AUTOSAR 4.0 specification describes MulShRight  as arithmetic shift right, which is equivalent to rounding to floor.  Given the preceding conditions, code replacement of a multiplication operation that rounds to zero with MulShRight  is incorrect.
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1435708 to download a patch that addresses this and other bugs.

Summary: MATLAB? might crash when the subplot function is interrupted with Ctrl or Command keys.
Description: Interrputing the execution of the subplot  function in the following ways might cause MATLAB to crash: Pressing Ctrl -C  on a Windows? system Pressing Command-Period (.)  on a Macintosh system  This crash typically occurs within a script or function involving multiple calls to the subplot  function.

Summary: Word reports based on Word templates that contain shapes in headers and footers cannot be opened
Description: Microsoft Word templates that contains shapes, especially when the shapes are in headers or footers, can cause an error when the report is opened in Word. For example, inserting a text box in a header when the text box is a built-in Facet Sidebar .

Summary: Stateflow charts using atomic subcharts with local data of Bus type may encounter unexpected errors
Description: Stateflow throws an error when a chart contains at least two levels of atomic subcharts, with a data store memory on the lowest level mapped to a local data object on the middle level, and the local data is of type Bus .  Error using tlc_c (line 179)     The index of the bus element is specified as '0'. This value is not valid for the region of memory 'blkpath/ SFunction ' associated with the block 'DataName' The error message may refer to a different data object, but it is the data store that is causing the problem.
Workaround: Change the scope of mid-level local data object to Output. You can connect the output port to a terminator block on the Simulink canvas if necessary.

Summary: MATLAB throws an error related to "jarego.jayatana" during startup
Description: Upon starting MATLAB in an Ubuntu environment with Unity global menu enabled, the following exception is thrown in the Linux command line: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.awt.IllegalComponentStateException: component must be showing on the screen to determine its location at java.awt.Component.getLocationOnScreen_NoTreeLock(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.getLocationOnScreen(Unknown Source) atcom.jarego.jayatana.swing.SwingGlobalMenuWindow.componentResized(SwingGlobalMenuWindow.java:549) at java.awt.AWTEventMulticaster.componentResized(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.processComponentEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.processEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Container.processEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Window.processEvent(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.mwswing.MJFrame.processEvent(MJFrame.java:868) at java.awt.Component.dispatchEventImpl(Unknown Source) In addition, accessing menu items does not work as expected.  This is happens because this new Ubuntu Uniti global menu feature interferes with MATLAB's Java features. Note that this Unity global menu feature is enabled by default starting in Ubuntu 15.04.
Workaround: Prior to starting MATLAB, check the JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS environment variable. If this variable contains the following string, remove it:-javaagent:/usr/share/java/jayatanaag.jarNote that you will need to do this step when starting MATLAB from a new Linux shell as this change only applies to the current shell.

Summary: Use of end in saturation limit of Integrator generates TLC error
Description: Code generation from a model displays an error if you use end  in the value of the Upper saturation limit  or Lower saturation limit  parameters of an Integrator block. For example, you set the value to myVector(end) . The error message resembles this example: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015a\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 1202 Column: 8 %exit directive:  Simulink Coder Fatal in block: "myModel/Integrator", block type "Integrator": Unrecognized Identifier: M_UNKNOWN
Workaround: Use the expression end^1 instead of end. For example, set the value of the saturation limit to myVector(end^1).?

Summary: Variant subsytems can trigger 'duplicate' or 'uniqueness' error for AUTOSAR VariationPointProxy objects
Description: Building or validating a model configured for the AUTOSAR target can generate a 'duplicate' or 'uniqueness' error if both of the following conditions exist: The model uses variant subsystems. A Simulink.Variant  object is used as a conditional in multiple subsystems.  Under these conditions, the first build succeeds, but subsequent attempts to validate or build the model fail with an error similar to one of these: The AUTOSAR short name MySimulinkVariant is a duplicate.  The short name 'MySimulinkVariant' (VariationPointProxy /pkg/A/MySimulinkVariant) failed uniqueness tests. The short name 'MySimulinkVariant' (VariationPointProxy /pkg/A/MySimulinkVariant) already exists. You should change one of these short names so that they are unique. The uniqueness tests are case sensitive.
Workaround: This workaround applies to R2014b and AUTOSAR support package v14.2.1. The workaround has two parts, with an attachment provided for each:Fix models exhibiting this issue.Prevent the issue from occurring with new models.Fix Models Exhibiting This IssueDownload and install attachment_1260670_deleteDuplicateVPPs_2015-06-19.zip. The provided deleteDuplicateVPPs() function will remove duplicate VariationPointProxy objects from a given model. This allows models exhibiting this issue to successfully validate and build.Prevent the Issue From Occurring with New ModelsDownload and install attachment_1260670_14b_2015-06-19.zip.Installation Instructions for AUTOSAR Support PackageLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your AUTOSAR installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your AUTOSAR installation by typing autosar_get_root_directory at the MATLAB command prompt. The path that precedes "toolbox" will be referred to as <AUTOSAR> in subsequent steps. For example, if autosar_get_root_directory returns "C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\autosarstandard\toolbox\coder\autosar", <AUTOSAR> will be "C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\autosarstandard".Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <AUTOSAR> folder. If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > GeneralMake a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <AUTOSAR>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <AUTOSAR> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <AUTOSAR>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <AUTOSAR> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <AUTOSAR> folder. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file(s).Extract the contents of the file(s) to your <AUTOSAR> folder.

Summary: Invalid transmission rate prescaler value was used for XCP Simulink communication
Description: If you run a simulation with Simulink for XCP communication, it uses a prescaler value of 0, which is an invalid value according to XCP specifications. If you run the Simulink model with the third-party XCP Sample implementation from Vector , the Vector  implementation automatically corrects the prescaler value to 1. If you run the Simulink model with another XCP implementation that does not correct the invalid prescaler value, the XCP application can run incorrectly.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when state names are longer than 25 characters
Description: In Stateflow charts using MATLAB as the Action Language, using state names longer than 25 characters might cause MATLAB to crash when simulating the model or generating code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code for AUTOSAR model with inport configured for ExplicitReceive data access
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for an AUTOSAR model if all of the following conditions are true: A root-level Inport block is connected to a Switch block in a subsystem. A Data Type Conversion block is present on the signal path between the root-level Inport block and the Switch block. In the AUTOSAR configuration of the model, the AUTOSAR data access mode of the root-level inport is set to ExplicitReceive . Summary: Importing frequency-domain data with zero sample time into the systemIdentification app fails.
Description: When you import frequency-domain data in either of the formats listed below and designate the data sample time to be zero, you get an error dialog indicating that the sample time value is invalid. The error dialog contains the following text:Invalid value specified for the sample time. For multi-experiment data use a cell array of doubles.   Formats affected by the bug: Frequency-domain signals   Frequency Function (Complex)   Frequency Function (Amplitude/Phase)
Workaround: If you want to import frequency-domain signals with zero sample time, create an IDDATA object beforehand and import the data as an object.If the output signal is Y, the input signal U and frequency W, then create an object as follows:data = iddata(Y, U, 0, 'Frequency', W);  If you want to import frequency response data with zero sample time, create an IDFRD object beforehand and import the data as an object.If H is the complex frequency response matrix and W is the frequency vector, create the IDFRD object as:data = idfrd(H, W, 0);If the data is available originally in amplitude/phase form, first transform the amplitude and phase information into a complex frequency response matrix by using the relationship H = AMP.*exp(1i*THETA) where AMP contains the amplitude data and THETA contains the phase data in radians.After creating the object (IDDATA or IDFRD), choose Import data -> Data object... option in the systemIdentification app and specify "data" in the Object edit box of the import dialog. Click Import.

Summary: Unsaved test harnesses are discarded if you attempt to save a model to a read-only directory
Description: Attempting to save a model to a directory for which you do not have write permission results in the following error: Error using save_system (line 38)Error writing file "C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\foo.slx":  copy failed: fl:filesystem:AccessDenied Permission denied  If the model you attempted to save contains test harnesses that were not previously saved, the harnesses are lost.

Summary: Link Editor dialog corrupts DOORS Surrogate Item links from Simulink
Description: When Simulink models synchronize with the DOORS surrogate module, and the Link Editor dialog is opened for a Simulink object that has a "DOORS Surrogate item" link, the surrogate link gets corrupted if either OK or Apply is pressed, even when no changes were made. Navigation from this Simulink object to the surrogate module will no longer work, unless the model is re-synchronized.
Workaround: For already corrupted instances: delete the corrupted link and re-synchronize the modelPermanent solution for future Link Editor invocations: install the attached patch:* back up toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/@ReqMgr/rmidlg_apply.p,* back up toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/@ReqMgr/rmidlg_apply.m, if present* unzip the attached patch file to the directory [MATLAB_INSTALL_DIR] (for example, C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015a)* restart MATLAB?

Summary: Parallel building of referenced models fails when measurement of function execution times is enabled
Description: Parallel building of referenced models fails if the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Verification > Measure function execution times  parameter is selected for at least one of the referenced models. You see an error, for example: ### Starting parallel model reference RTW build for 'child3'### The following error occurred during the parallel model reference RTW build for 'child3': Could not open file child3.h. No such file or directory. ? ? ? ?
Workaround: Do not use parallel building of referenced models when function execution time measurement is enabled. For the top model of the model reference hierarchy, on the Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing pane, clear the Enable parallel model reference builds check box.?

Summary: Datetime plot with rotated tick labels lags on zoom when Property Inspector is open
Description: A datetime plot rescales twice on each zooming operation if it has rotated tick labels along the datetime axis and the Property Inspector is open. 1. Create a plot with a datetime variable on the x-axis: dtArray = datetime(2015,6,30) + days(1:10);plot(dtArray, randn(size(dtArray))); 2. Rotate the tick label along the datetime  axis: ax = gca;ax.XTickLabelRotation = 45; 3. Open the Property Inspector 4. Zoom in on the datetime  plot Notice the axis rescales twice on each zooming operation.
Workaround: Close the Property Inspector. After it is closed, zooming operations will function normally.

You will see unexpected results in HDL simulation or FPGA implementation due to the rate discrepancy.

Summary: Code inspection returns an error if the code folder is a short file name in Windows
Description: When you inspect your generated code with Simulink Code Inspector, if you specify a code folder name that is a Windows generated short file name, following the MS-DOS 8.3 naming convention, code inspection returns the following hard error: Error reading $LONGPATHNAME:$LINENUM in Results.codeTable.Data Inspection Message: The specified entry is not present in the dictionary . $LONGPATHNAME  is the corresponding long file name, and $LINENUM  is a code location in the generated code.
Workaround: When you specify a code folder name during inspection, use the long file name version of the short file name.

Summary: canMessageImport might not import all messages from Vector log files
Description: When reading Vector log files with canMessageImport  in Vehicle Network Toolbox, some messages might be skipped for import. This includes messages where no space is present at the beginning of a line or where the channel number for the message is 3 or higher.

Summary: Signals become NaN (not-a-number) when a model contains more than one function-call subsystem triggered by I/O interrupts
Description: When multiple function-call subsystems driven by I/O interrupts are used in a model, signals can become NaN.
Workaround: Update to an R2015b or newer version, or contact Mathworks technical support for a R2014a, R2014b, or R2015a patch.

Summary: Error when using HMI blocks connected to a linked library subsystem with Stateflow blocks
Description: If an HMI block in a Simulink model is connected to a block inside a linked library subsystem that also contains a Stateflow block, then Stateflow will not work properly with the model. If you try to do a model update, Simulink may throw either of the following error messages: Error calling slsfnagctlr('ClearSimulation',...)No appropriate method, property, or field 'Id' for class 'Stateflow.Machine'. Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. If you try to save the model, Simulink throws the following error: failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\stateflow\fsm\machine.cpp at line 632:mxIsNumeric(prhs[0]) && mxGetLength(prhs[0]) == 1 It will not be possible to save the model.

Summary: Using an rfdata.nf object to specify the 'NoiseData' property of an rfckt.amplifier or an rfckt.mixer yields incorrect cascaded results.
Description: There is a bug in how the RF Toolbox calculates cascaded noise figure when rfdata.nf  objects are used. When creating an rfckt.amplifier  or an rfckt.mixer , you can specify a noise figure in two ways. Either as a scalar value (that represents a frequency-independent noise figure) or as an rfdata.nf  object. When using the same noise figure values, these two approaches should yield the same results, but they do not for cascades of more than one stage. This is a bug. The following code illustrates the bug: nfdb = [25 13]; % Noise figure of each stage in dB data = read(rfdata.data,'samplebjt2.s2p'); % Note: no noise is in this file freq = data.Freq; net = rfdata.network('Freq',freq,'Data',data.S_Parameters,'Z0',data.Z0); % Compare a chain of two amps with NoiseData set to a scalar versus two % amps woth NoiseData set to rfdata.nf objects % Build a chain using scalar noise figurescalaramp1 = rfckt.amplifier('NetworkData',net,'NoiseData',nfdb(1),'NonlinearData',Inf); scalaramp2 = rfckt.amplifier('NetworkData',net,'NoiseData',nfdb(2),'NonlinearData',Inf); scalarcasc = rfckt.cascade('Ckts',{scalaramp1,scalaramp2}); analyze(scalarcasc,freq); % Build a chain using "rfdata.nf" objectsnf1 = rfdata.nf('Freq',freq,'Data',nfdb(1)*ones(numel(freq),1)); objamp1 = rfckt.amplifier('NetworkData',net,'NoiseData',nf1,'NonlinearData',Inf); nf2 = rfdata.nf('Freq',freq,'Data',nfdb(2)*ones(numel(freq),1)); objamp2 = rfckt.amplifier('NetworkData',net,'NoiseData',nf2,'NonlinearData',Inf); objcasc = rfckt.cascade('Ckts',{objamp1,objamp2}); analyze(objcasc,freq); % Compare resulting noise figures. Should be zero sum(abs(scalarcasc.AnalyzedResult.NF - objcasc.AnalyzedResult.NF))

Summary: Incorrect range on variable using '-03' option
Description: Consider the following pseudo code. In your verification results, if you place your cursor on a , the tooltip displays a wrong range 0..10. Because b  has range -10..10, the range of a  following the assignment must also be -10..10. /* at init */option -03typedef long s32; s32 a is full range s32 b has range -10..10 s32 c has range 1..2 s32 bar(void) {  a = (b < 0) ? -( ((-b) / c)) : (b / c);  s32 res = b; return res;}  The range of b  is also affected. If you place your cursor on b  in the line s32 res=b , the tooltip displays a wrong range 0..10.
Workaround: There are two possible work arounds:Relaunch verification using option -02. This corresponds to running verification at precision level 2 instead of 3. The correct ranges appear at this precision level. Note: -02 (precision level 2) is the default precision level.Or follow installation instructions belowInstallation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Figures cut off in HTML when publishing using headless Linux
Description: If you start MATLAB on Linux systems with "-nodesktop ¨CnoFigureWindows" or "-nodisplay" and then publish a MATLAB file that contains figures, the figures generated on the HTML page are cut off.
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1349402 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues that exist in MATLAB R2015a and R2015aSP1.Visit Bug Report ID 1349473 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues in R2015a and R2015aSP1 compiled applications.

Summary: Subsystem variants with Function packaging set to Inline might cause uncompilable code
Description: When all of the following conditions are met, the code generator might incorrectly place the subsystem function return statement: A model contains a subsystem that encloses a variant subsystem. There is one outport port from the variant subsystem and the subsystem that encloses the variant subsystem. For the subsystem variants, the Function packaging  is set to Inline . For the subsystem that encloses the variant subsystem, the Function packaging  is set to Nonreusable function  and the Function interface  is set to Allow arguments .  For example, in the attached model (VariantSubsystemReturn.slx), the generated code is uncompilable because of a misplaced return statement in the subsystem function.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:If you want to leverage the subsystem function return value, set the Function packaging for the variant subsystems to Nonreusable function and the Function interface to Allow arguments.If you do not want to leverage the substem function return value, set the Function packaging for the variant subsystems to Nonreusable function and the Function interface to void_void.

Summary: MuPAD notebook can cause MATLAB to hang on Mac OS Yosemite 10.10.3
Description: In rare cases that depend on your hardware and software configuration executing a command in the MuPAD notebook on Mac OS X Yosemite 10.10.3 causes MATLAB to hang. If this problem exists on your hardware and software configuration, it shows up as follows: You start a MuPAD notebook - for example, by typing mupad  in the MATLAB Command Window. In the MuPAD notebook, you try a simple computation - for example, you type 2+3 . The computation does not return any value, and MATLAB hangs.  If you encounter this problem, please contact our technical support at https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html . ?

Any function that depends on the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/rflib  directory is affected. This is a significant percentage of RF Toolbox functionality. After implementing the workaround below, a working executable is built with the following command: mcc('-m','testsparamdeploy.m','-a',fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox','shared','rflib'))

Summary: Exporting a Test Harness discards harness model parameters
Description: Exporting a test harness discards the model parameters for the harness model and replaces them with default parameters. Therefore, saved parameters are lost when a test harness is exported to a separate model.  This behavior occurs for both ways of exporting a test harness:Selecting File > Export Model To > Independent Model  Using the sltest.harness.export  function

Summary: Error when simulating or generating code after switching between variants with Stateflow charts
Description: Models using linked variant subsystems in which one variant contains Stateflow charts and the other does not can produce the following error during simulation or code generation after the active variant is switched to one in which there are no Stateflow charts within the variant subsystem.  Unable to open block template file lib_MODEL_NAME .tlc from GENERATE or GENERATE_TYPE.
Workaround: After switching the active variant, delete the slprj folder and the generated mex file (if any) associated with the model; this will regenerate code for the entire model. You may need to execute the clear mex command to unload the mex file from memory before deleting it.

Summary: ncread returns incorrect dimension lengths for some netCDF-4 files
Description: When reading a netCDF-4 file, the ncread  function might incorrectly extract the dimension lengths of a multidimensional variable.  This is due to a bug in the underlying netCDF library version 4.1.3.

Summary: LibCreateSourceFileAtPath ignores basename argument
Description: The TLC function LibCreateSourceFileAtPath  takes two arguments that describe filenames, namewithpath  and basename . One use of the basename  argument might be to name a header guard in the emitted files. When you pass a basename  argument to the LibCreateSourceFileAtPath  function, the code generator ignores the basename  argument and instead uses the namewithpath  argument. ?

Summary: Slow analysis performance for models with many Data Store Memory blocks
Description: If your model contains many Data Store Memory blocks, this can negatively impact Simulink Design Verifier analysis performance.

Summary: fft function can generate wrong answer for gpuArray of row vectors or when DIM is not 1
Description: When calling fft  with a gpuArray , the answer is incorrect if all of the following conditions are met:  You perform the fft  across the columns or a higher dimension of your data. This occurs when your data is a row vector, or the third argument DIM  > 1. Your signal length size(X,DIM)  is at least 131. For R2014b and R2015a, your signal length is at most 1021 (for doubles) or 4093 (for singles); for R2014a there is no upper bound. Your signal length has a prime factor over 127.  For example, if your data X  is an N-by-M gpuArray  of underlying class double, and you compute the fft  as follows    Y = fft(X,[],2); then in R2015a Y  is incorrect for 293 values of  M = {131, 137, 139, 149, ..., 1018, 1019, 1021} .
Workaround: Permute your data so that you are performing the fft down the columns of your array.Ideally, keep your data this way throughout. If this is not possible, then permute the result back.If X is a row vector, or a 2-D matrix, and you are performing the N-point fft along its rows, use a transpose:   Y = fft(X',N)';For N-D arrays with the signal along dimension DIM, use permute as follows:   permuteVec = [DIM:ndims(X) 1:(DIM-1)];  X = permute(X,permuteVec);  Y = fft(X,N);  Y = ipermute(Y,permuteVec);To determine whether your gpuArray needs to be permuted, use the function needsPermute from the attached zip file.   if needsPermute(X,DIM)      % Use workaround  else      % Normal FFT  endYou can instead choose to permute all inputs, which typically has a small cost for large arrays. Use gputimeit to determine which approach is fastest.The attachment also includes the fftGPUWorkaround function. This implements these described changes, and you can substitute it wherever you call fft.

Summary: Cannot sort libraries to the top of the Library Browser list
Description: In the Library Browser, you cannot sort libraries above the Simulink library. The Simulink library has a default sort priority of -1 , so you should be able to sort libraries above the Simulink library by setting its priority to -2 . However, this does not work.
Workaround: In your sl_customization.m file, explicitly set the sort priority of the Simulink library to -1. For example:function sl_customization(cm) cm.LibraryBrowserCustomizer.applyOrder( {'My Library', -2, 'Simulink', -1}); end

Summary: MATLAB can crash when rendering text objects with large strings or a large number of text objects
Description: MATLAB can crash when rendering a single text object with a large string or when rendering a large number of text objects. Due to the limitations on the JAVA heap memory size, text objects under one or more of the following conditions can fail to allocate requisite JAVA heap memory and eventually crash MATLAB: Excessively large font size  Excessively large number of characters in the string (~25000)  Excessively large number of text objects in the figure
Workaround: In most cases, this bug is encountered accidentally through code error. If you did not intend to create the large text objects, debug your code to identify the error. For more information on debugging code, see:https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/debugging-code.html If you intended to render the large text objects, consider increasing the available JAVA Heap memory. For more information on increasing the size of the JAVA Heap memory, see:https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_external/java-heap-memory-preferences.html

Summary: Loading a figure with a datetime plot and legend shifts the datetime ticks
Description: When you load a figure file that contains a datetime plot and legend, the datetime ticks are shifted. 1. Create a plot with a datetime variable on the X-axis and add a legend: t = datetime(2011,1,1):1/24:datetime(2011,12,31); plot(t,sin((1:length(t))/length(t)*pi))legend('datetime')  2. Save the figure. 3. Load the figure. The ticks are shifted from their original positions and are difficult to read.
Workaround: Zoom in or out on the figure by using the Zoom In or Zoom Out tool. Then right click and select Reset to Original View from the drop-down menu to correct the tick positioning. Alternatively, you can change the X-axis limits of the figure using the Pan tool.

Summary: The ispassive function is invalid for S-parameter data referenced to a complex impedance
Description: For releases R2015a and earlier, the ispassive  function assumes the input S-parameter data is referenced to a real, positive impedance. For example, the following code returns the expected result (true): s1 = [0 0;1 0]; % Referenced to 50 ohmsispassive(s1)  Converting s1  to any other impedance should not change the passivity. Below, s1  is converted to a complex reference impedance using the s2s  function. ispassive  should return the same result on the new data, but it does not: z1 = 50;z2 = 25 + 25i;s2 = s2s(s1,z1,z2);ispassive(s2)  This is a bug. When the S-parameter data is referenced to a complex impedance, ispassive  may give incorrect results.
Workaround: Use the s2s function to convert the S-parameters to reference a positive, real impedance. For example:s11 = 0.2 - 0.4i;s12 = 0;s21 = 1.12 + 0.16i;s22 = 0.2 - 0.4i;z_complex = 25 + 25i;s_complex = [s11 s12;s21 s22]; % Referenced to 25 + 25i, should be passiveispassive(s_complex) % ispassive gives incorrect resultz_real = 50;s_real = s2s(s_complex,z_complex,z_real); % Convert to 50 ohmsdisp(s_real)ispassive(s_real) % ispassive gives correct result

Summary: Unable to open MATLAB? Coder? app project in earlier release after opening in R2015a?
Description: In R2015a, if you open a  project from an earlier release, the MATLAB?  Coder? app updates the project file to the R2015a format. Because of this format change, you cannot open the project in the earlier release. ?
Workaround: In R2015a, before you use the MATLAB Coder app to open a project from an earlier release, create a backup of the project file. Use the backup project file in the earlier release.

Summary: Overriding signal logging with large number of signals can slow down simulation significantly
Description: If you override signal logging when a large number of signals are configured to log, simulation can be quite slow. The simulation can get stuck in the 'initialization' phase for an extended period of time. Overriding signals involves one  of these actions: Use the Signal Logging Selector to override signals. From the command line, create a ModelLoggingInfo  object, using the Simulink.SimulationData.ModelLoggingInfo.createFromModel  API, modify some of the settings on signals in the Signals  property, and then set the model to use this override: set_param('model', 'DataLoggingOverride', mli)
Workaround: In some cases, simulating without overriding signals can be faster.  Consider simulating without overriding any logged signals.The following are instructions for a workaround for release R2015a:Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Windows Systems*:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  *Notes for Windows Systems:      You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.    If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you  may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Summary: Model block SIL or PIL simulation can generate code for unrelated referenced models
Description: Simulating a Model block in the software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) mode, with the Model block Code interface  parameter set to Model reference , can trigger code generation for unrelated referenced components. The unnecessary code generation occurs when the block path of the unrelated referenced component contains the name of a referenced model that is in SIL or PIL mode. Consider a top model, Logic_Harness , which contains Model blocks A  and B. In addition: A  references the Logic  model. B  references the UDP_SendReceive  model.  Simulating Logic_Harness/A  in SIL or PIL mode triggers an unnecessary build of UDP_SendReceive . This build occurs because Logic_Harness  contains the substring Logic . This issue can lead to: Longer build times for SIL and PIL simulations. Errors if the unrelated referenced components do not fully support code generation.
Workaround: Rename models. Make sure that the names of referenced models simulating in SIL or PIL mode are not substrings of the block path of any other Model block. For the example above, you can change the name of the Logic_Harness model to Harness.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when?passing an empty character array to the?'%s'?format specifier of?fprintf
Description: If you pass a fixed-size empty character array to the fprintf  format specifier '%s'  in?your MATLAB code, the generated code behaves incorrectly. For example,?the?MATLAB code could contain this expression: fprintf('%s','')  The incorrect behavior?can be printing out something like '(null)' , printing out garbled data, or a runtime crash. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: n-D Lookup Table blocks with different integer rounding modes in the same model can generate incorrect code
Description: n-D Lookup Table blocks in a model can generate incorrect code if all  of these conditions are met. The data types for the various parameters of the n-D Lookup Table block are in the Data Types  tab in the block parameters dialog box. The model has multiple almost identical n-D Lookup Table blocks with different settings for Integer rounding mode . The data type of the Fraction  parameter is a floating-point type. The data type of the Intermediate results  parameter has a higher precision than the data type of the Table data  parameter. The data type of the Output  parameter is an integer or fixed-point type. The data type of the Output  and Intermediate results  parameters are different. The data type of the Output  parameter has a lower precision than the datatype of the Intermediate results  parameter. Index search method  is set to Linear search  or Binary search .  In this case, all the blocks call only one function, which uses the integer rounding mode of the first n-D Lookup Table block.
Workaround: In the n-D Lookup Table block, set the data type of the Output parameter to be the same as the Intermediate results data type. Use a Data Type Conversion block to cast the output of the n-D Lookup Table block to the required type with the desired integer rounding mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Integer overflow may be incorrectly missing from generated code
Description: For a model design that results in generated code in which an integer is cast to a smaller integer type, so that overflow occurs, the generated code may be missing the overflow, resulting in wrong answers.

Summary: MATLAB crash due to model argument data type mismatch
Description: When you generate code from a model, including for accelerated simulations, MATLAB can crash if all of these conditions are true: The model uses a Model block to reference another model. The referenced model uses model arguments. An argument value that you specify in the Model block appears in the generated code as a tunable variable. For example, the value is tunable if you set the configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  to Tunable  in the parent model, or if you set the value by using a Simulink.Parameter  object that uses a storage class other than Auto . The data type of the argument value that you specify in the Model block differs from the data type that the argument uses in the referenced model.  The data type that the argument uses in the referenced model can depend on either of these specifications: The data type of the variable in the referenced model workspace. The usage of the argument in blocks in the referenced model. The code generator applies context-sensitive data typing if the variable in the model workspace uses the data type double  or if the variable is a parameter object whose data type is auto .
Workaround: Resolve the data type mismatch. For example, suppose that:In the Model block, you set the value of the model argument to int32(311).In the referenced model workspace, the model argument is a Simulink.Parameter object whose data type is auto.In a Constant block in the referenced model, you use the model argument as the value of the Constant value parameter. You set the output data type of the block to int16.To resolve the data type mismatch, you can use one of these techniques:In the Model block, set the value of the model argument to int16(311). In the generated code, the argument uses the data type int16.Set the output data type of the Constant block to int32. In the generated code, the argument uses the data type int32.Set the output data type of the Constant block to Inherit: Inherit from 'Constant value'. Set the data type of the parameter object in the model workspace to int16. Set the value of the model argument in the Model block to 311. In the generated code, the argument uses the data type int16.

Summary: Code inspection can return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" when a Switch Case Action Subsystem redefines vector signal
Description: Simulink Code Inspector can return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" when a Switch Case Action Subsystem redefines a vector signal under both of these conditions: The Switch Case Action Subsystem contains more than two cases. Each case redefines only a part of the vector signal. Summary: MATLAB Production Server documentation states that mps-profile is introduced in R2014b
Description: The MATLAB Production Server documentation has a section on the mps-profile  tool, and states that it is introduced in R2014b. The tool will actually be introduced in a later version and is not available in R2014b or R2015a.

Summary: Ethercat SDO blocks work only if the network ID is 0
Description: If the device ID is anything other than 0 , these Ethercat SDO blocks error and do not function correctly: EtherCAT Sync SDO Upload EtherCAT Async SDO Upload EtherCAT Sync SDO Download EtherCAT Async SDO Download  ?

Summary: Appending a nonscalar DOM object array silently fails
Description: Appending an array of DOM objects containing more than one DOM object appends only the first object in the array. There is no message indicating that the other objects are ignored. For example, the report generated by the following script contains only "Hello." import mlreportgen.dom.*;d = Document('myrpt');ta = Text.empty();ta(1,1) = Text('Hello ');ta(2,1) = Text('World');p = Paragraph();append(p, ta);append(d, p);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: The output of datetime contains periods at the end of short names for days and months for certain non-English locales
Description: When you create strings from a datetime array, using the char  or cellstr  functions, you can specify a locale to control the language in which those strings are created. For certain non-English locales, the short (abbreviated) names for days and months are created with periods at the end of each name. The following are examples showing the discrepancy in the output for a few languages: Spanish    >> char(datetime('2014-03-26'), 'eee, dd-MMM-yyyy', 'es_ES')  ans =  mi¨¦., 26-mar.-2014  Italian    >> char(datetime('2014-03-26'), 'eee, dd-MMM-yyyy', 'it_IT')  ans =  mer, 26-mar-2014  Dutch    >> char(datetime('2014-03-26'), 'eee, dd-MMM-yyyy', 'nl_NL')  ans =  wo, 26-mrt.-2014  French    >> char(datetime('2014-03-26'), 'eee, dd-MMM-yyyy', 'fr_FR')  ans =  mer., 26-mars-2014

Summary: Embedded Coder can fail to generate code for AUTOSAR models containing a Stateflow chart
Description: Embedded Coder can fail to generate code for an AUTOSAR model that has the following modeling pattern:  The model contains a Stateflow chart using a Stateflow library chart. The library chart input is connected to a root Inport block of the model. The Inport block feeds a root Outport block of the model.  The root Outport block is mapped to an AUTOSAR explicit send port.  When Embedded Coder fails to generate code due to this bug, it issues a error message like the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2014b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc Line: 1926 Column: 33 Unable to find VarGroupIdx within the GlobalBlockOutput scope "
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block before the Stateflow chart Inport or before the root Outport block. In the Signal Conversion block, enable the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization option.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect C file placement of entry-point functions when single-file partitioning and example main generation are enabled
Description: When you generate standalone libraries or executables for multiple entry-point functions, if single-file partioning and example main generation are both enabled, MATLAB? Coder? might place some of the entry-point functions in the example main file instead of the primary C file. The example main file main.c  , located in the examples  subfolder of the build folder, is a template that shows you how to call the entry-point functions. By default, example main generation is enabled.
Workaround: If you do not want the example main file, you can avoid this problem by disabling example main generation.If you want the example main file: Set the partitioning method to multi-file partitioning and enable example main generation.Generate code. MATLAB? Coder? generates the correct main.c file.To preserve the main.c file, copy it to a folder outside of the build folder. Set the partitioning method to single-file partioning  and disable example main generation. MATLAB? Coder? generates the correct application code.To disable example main generation:At the command line:       cfg = coder.config('lib'); % or dll or exe       cfg.GenerateExampleMain = 'DoNotGenerate';Using the MATLAB? Coder? app:              In the project settings dialog box, on the All Settings tab, under Advanced, set Generate example main to Do not generate an example main function.To set the file partioning method:At the command line, set the FilePartionMethod property to 'SingleFile' or 'MapMFileToCFile' (default).       cfg = coder.config('lib'); % or dll or exe       cfg.FilePartionMethod = 'SingleFile';Using the MATLAB? Coder? app:               In the project settings dialog box, on the Code Appearance tab, set Generated file partitioning method to Generate all functions into a single file or Generate one file for each MATLAB file (default).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The duration operator used in a Test Sequence block within an enabled subsystem does not ignore the time during which the subsystem is disabled if the solver is variable step
Description: Within an enabled subsystem, if a variable step solver is selected in the configuration parameters for the model, any use of the duration  operator within that subsystem fails to ignore the time during which the subsystem is disabled. For example, consider the following statement, used inside a test sequence block within an enabled subsystem: y = duration(true);   Assume that the subsystem is enabled between time t = 0  and time t = 5 , then disabled between time t = 5  and time t = 10 , and then enabled again between time t = 10  and t = 15 . Using a variable-step solver gives this incorrect result: y = 15  Using a fixed-step solver, the operator returns the correct answer: y = 10
Workaround: Use a fixed-step solver.

Summary: shapewrite fails when FieldDecimalCount field of DBF spec is set to 0
Description: When using a DBF spec with FieldDecimalCount  set to 0 to specify feature attributes with floating-point values to be written as integers, shapewrite  issues the following error: Error using ==  Matrix dimensions must agree.
Workaround: Before writing the data to a shapefile, round the attribute values to the nearest integer.

Summary: From File block leaks memory when loading underspecified bus signal data
Description: When you load a bus signal using a From File block, you can specify a subset of leaf signals. For the rest of the leaf signals (underspecified signals), Simulink uses corresponding ground values. Each underspecified signal leaks a small amount of memory. A large number of underspecified signals can cause a substantial leak. The leaked memory is not released until the MATLAB process is finished.
Workaround: Provide data for each leaf signal when loading a bus signal using a From File block.

Blocks affected by this bug include, but are not limited to, Delay Enabled Delay Resettable Delay Variable Integer Delay Interpolation Using Prelookup Entity Departure Counter Entity Departure Function-Call Generator Entity Sink Event-Based Entity Generator FIFO Queue Get Attribute Infinite Server Input Switch Priority Queue LIFO Queue N-Server Output Switch Path Combiner Resource Acquire ResourcePool Resource Release Set Attribute Single Server Signal-Based Function-Call Generator Time-Based Function-Call Generator Time-Based Entity Generator
