Summary: Appending an empty DOM object array crashes MATLAB
Description: Appending an empty array of DOM objects to a document or another DOM object crashes MATLAB. For example, the following script crashes: import mlreportgen.dom.*;ta = Text().empty();p = Paragraph();append(p, ta);

Summary: SIL with BullseyeCoverage does not show coverage results for _sharedutils code
Description: To collect code coverage data for generated code, you can run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation that uses the BullseyeCoverage tool. You can view code coverage results using the BullseyeCoverage browser or the code generation report. However, the code generation report does not display code coverage annotations for generated code in the _sharedutils  folder. You can still view code coverage results for code in the _sharedutils  folder using the BullseyeCoverage browser. However, the browser displays results for only the most recent simulation. The browser does not display cumulative results.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier does not support Lookup Tables with Nearest Interpolation Method
Description: On models containing Lookup Tables with Nearest  Interpolation method, Design Verifier analysis does not provide information about the unsupported block and produces the following error message:  Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: < model > is incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier.
Workaround: Change the Interpolation Method of affected Lookup Table blocks.

Summary: Slow operations in the MATLAB? Coder? app
Description: The MATLAB Coder app checks entry-point functions for code generation readiness. For some large entry-point functions, or functions with many calls, these checks can take a long time. If the checks take a long time, certain app operations can be slower than expected, or appear to be unresponsive.

Summary: XCP Configuration block cannot connect to XCP slave with extended CAN ID
Description: When executing a Simulink model that contains an XCP Configuration block, communication with the XCP slave cannot be established if the A2L file defines an extended CAN ID for the XCP master and slave. If the Output connection status  is enabled for the XCP Configuration block, the connection status output remains 0. Use the following MATLAB commands to determine if an extended CAN ID is used in the A2L file for the XCP master and slave: >> a2lfile = xcpA2L('myfile.a2l') ;>> a2lfile.TransportLayerCANInfoans =  BaudRate: 500000 CANIDMaster: 1 CANIDMasterIsExtended: 1 CANIDSlave: 2 CANIDSlaveIsExtended: 1 ?

Summary: Missing first character in anchor label when creating RMI link to HTML file via Document Index tab of Link Editor
Description: RMI allows linking to an anchor in an HTML file by entering the filename and the anchor string in the Requirements Traceability > Link Editor dialog. The Document Index tab allows you to select the desired anchor from a list of anchors in a given HTML file. Links created via Document Index selection are operational, and look correct in Requirements Traceability context menus, but generated Requirements Traceability reports incorrectly strip the first character from anchor strings for links created via the Document Index tab of the Link Editor dialog.
Workaround: Workaround 1:  do not rely on Document Index selection to automatically populate link attributes when creating links to HTML files.  Instead, manually enter the desired anchor string into the Location ID field of Link Editor dialog.Workaround 2:  save copies of the following file:  [MATLAB_INSTALL_DIR]/toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/private/linktype_rmi_html.*,  and then extract the patched version from the attached zip file.   Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Inaccessible Model Advisor checks and functionality for seperately-installed MathWorks products
Description: If you install a MathWorks toolbox or blockset separately after you have installed Simulink, then when Model Advisor tries to perform checks, it incorrectly assumes that the installed toolbox or blockset is not available. In that case:  Model Advisor checks provided with the seperately-installed product are not available. Model Advisor does not perform checks that depend on the separately-installed product.
Workaround: In your MATLAB preferences folder, delete the maproductinfo.mat cache file. To find the the preferences folder, at the MATLAB command line, enter prefdir.Restart MATLAB and open the Model Advisor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Some calls to Simulink functions from a Stateflow chart might result in incorrect results
Description: If there is a call to a Simulink function from a Stateflow chart and the Stateflow chart is in a loop with other Simulink blocks, the call to the Simulink function might be ignored during simulation. During code generation, this construct might result in uncompilable code.?

Summary: Overwriting a run with plotted signals in the Simulation Data Inspector might crash MATLAB
Description: Stale plots might be left in the Simulation Data Inspector  after simulating a model if all of the following conditions are true: A previous run exists in the Simulation Data Inspector  Some logged signals of the run are checked for plotting The run is marked for Overwriting The new run does not log at least one of the plotted signals  Clicking on these stale plots might crash MATLAB

Summary: ModelSim crashes with large HDL Coder test benches
Description: When HDL Coder generates an HDL test bench with a sufficiently large number of test vectors, the generated HDL can cause ModelSim, a third-party HDL simulator, to crash on Linux. The number of test vectors that cause this fault is variable and depends on your environment.
Workaround: Consider generating a test bench that places the test vector data in external data files, by selecting the Configuration Parameters > HDL Code Generation Test Bench > Use file I/O to read/write test bench data checkbox. Alternatively, at the command line, enter: hdlset_param(model_name,'UseFileIOInTestbench','on')If this workaround is not possible or desired, you can also work around the problem by adjusting the Linux shell environment from which you launch MATLAB, by increasing available stack space. The command to use is specific to the command shell you use.For bash/sh:$ ulimit -s <value_in_kb> For tcsh/csh:% limit stack <value_in_kb> The value for the stack size that will allow a simulation to succeed can must be determined by experimentation. Doubling the current value is a reasonable value to start with.

Summary: DLMREAD outputs extra columns when data contains whitespace characters.
Description: DLMREAD erroneously adds extra columns to its output when the file contains whitespace characters.Example:fname = 'example.txt';afile = fopen(fname,'w');fprintf(afile,'A B,1,2\n');fclose(afile);out = dlmread(fname, ',', 0, 1)out =     1     2     0 The correct output is:out =     1     2

Summary: MATLAB crash on selecting deleted AUTOSAR runnable in Simulink-AUTOSAR Mapping Explorer
Description: The Simulink-AUTOSAR Mapping Explorer incorrectly displays a deleted AUTOSAR runnable if both of the following conditions exist: Your AUTOSAR model contains an AUTOSAR runnable that is configured to be triggered by an OperationInvokedEvent . In the AUTOSAR Properties Explorer, you delete the runnable for which the OperationInvokedEvent  is defined.  Under these conditions, the deletion of the runnable might not correctly update the list of available runnables in the Simulink-AUTOSAR Mapping Explorer. The Entry Point Functions view might still display the deleted runnable. Selecting the deleted runnable crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your AUTOSAR installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your AUTOSAR installation by typing autosar_get_root_directory at the MATLAB command prompt. The path that precedes "toolbox" will be referred to as <AUTOSAR> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.The attached workarounds are specific to Embedded Coder Support Package for AUTOSAR version 15.11.0. Check your installed support package version using the command matlabshared.supportpkg.getInstalled, then download attachment whose filename contains the matching version number, e.g. attachment_1233633_autosar_spkg_15_11_0_2016_05_10.zip. Store the file in the <AUTOSAR> folder. Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <AUTOSAR> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <AUTOSAR> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac): unzip -l <AUTOSAR>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file into the <AUTOSAR> folder. Summary: Compiler warning or error when chart calls a Simulink function defined in a referenced model
Description: A compiler warning or error might occur when compiling code for a model in which: A Stateflow chart in a parent model calls a Simulink function. The Simulink function is defined by a Simulink Function block in a referenced model.  When the generated code for the parent model is compiled: The compiler is unable to find a function declaration corresponding to the Simulink Function block. The compiler assumes an implicit function declaration. Depending on your compiler flags and the default behavior of your compiler, the compiler might generate a warning, generate an error, or continue with no message.  If compilation does not halt with an error, no corrective action is needed.
Workaround: To work around a compiler error or warning, in the parent model, you can manually specify the prototype of the function corresponding to the Simulink Function block in the referenced model. Use Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Custom Code > Header file to specify custom code to include near the top of the generated model header file.

Summary: Error 'Lack of cast between incompatible source operand types' in internal phase
Description: When you run a Polyspace verification on C code, the verification may fail during the intermediate language translation phase with the following error message: **** C to intermediate language translation - 3 (P_SIA)  ----------------------------------------------------------------------  @FFT: Lack of cast between incompatible source operand types in PROC P.myfunction  instr : e1: seq t:g4 (i.32) myfunction.rb1{["source coordinates": file.c  | 15  | 23854]}, e2  |  | e2: ldc t:g194 (enum.8) 0  |An internal consistency problem has been detected.  |In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,  |the computation has been halted.  The following pseudo-code can generate this error. In this example, the switch  statement uses a ternary operator on enum -type variables: typedef enum { a = 0, b = 1 } my_enum;  ...void foo(my_enum m1, my_enum m2) {...switch ((m1 ? m2)? m1 : m2) { ... }...}
Workaround: For the previous pseudo-example, you can workaround this error by casting the expression inside the switch() to int.typedef enum { a = 0, b = 1 } my_enum; ...void foo(my_enum m1, my_enum m2) {...switch ((int)((m1 ? m2)? m1 : m2)) { ... }...}

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier displays error on models containing For Each Subsystem blocks with multiple mask parameters
Description: On models containing For Each Subsystem blocks with more than one mask parameter, Design Verifier displays the following error message:  Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated C code for Simulink function call array arguments using AUTOSAR schema version 3.x
Description: Embedded Coder generates incorrect C code for a model configured for the AUTOSAR target if both of the following conditions exist: In the model, a Simulink Function Caller block calls a Simulink function with an array argument. AUTOSAR schema version 3.x is selected for the model.  Under these conditions, the model build generates function call argument code that is appropriate for schema version 4.x, but not appropriate for 3.x. For example: real_T (*tmp)[2];...Rte_Call_cport_Fcn(rtb_callFcn, *tmp); For AUTOSAR schema version 3.x, array arguments are passed as pointer to array type rather than pointer to base type. The function call should not dereference the pointer. For example: real_T (*tmp)[2]; ... Rte_Call_cport_Fcn(rtb_callFcn, tmp); Depending on compiler warning and error settings, compilation might generate an error, or might implicitly cast from one pointer type to another and not trigger an error.

Summary: Moore chart using MATLAB as the Action Language and fixed-point values throws a simulation error in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: If you have fixed-point values in a Moore chart that uses MATLAB as the Action Language, Stateflow displays an error when you try to simulate the model in Rapid Accelerator mode:  Unable to inline '[location]'. It might be due to recursive calls.
Workaround: If you created your Moore chart in R2014b or prior, change the State Machine Type in the chart properties from Moore to Classic. State actions are updated accordingly to "during, exit:". This workaround preserves the behavior of the model as it was in those prior releases.Otherwise, work around this issue by changing the Action Language in the chart properties from MATLAB to C.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Error check for dynamic memory allocation on handle class objects might not occur resulting in wrong answer
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if the following conditions hold:? You store handle objects in persistent variables You create new handle objects when the program is run, and these newly created objects are stored in the persistent variable repeatedly  The error check that is supposed to detect and report these unsupported cases fails and results in code that produces wrong answers. A more detailed explanation follows. MATLAB Coder currently does not support dynamic memory allocation for handle class objects. When dynamic memory allocation is required for handle class objects, MATLAB Coder must error.? Consider this example where MATLAB Coder produces an error. The function creates multiple handle class objects inside a loop. The code generator has no information on which handle object is made persistent at compile time. Therefore the code generator stores each object and uses dynamic memory allocation because the number of objects is unbounded. function y = myloop(n, j)?? for i = 1:n?  ? ? h = my_handle_object();  ? ? subfcn(i, j, h);?  end?  function y = subfcn(i, j, h)?  persistent p;?  if i == j?  ? ? p = h;?  end?  To determine whether dynamic memory allocation is needed, MATLAB Coder attempts to detect for-loops and detect where handle class objects might survive outside for-loops, such as in persistent variables, and thereby require dynamic memory allocation to be stored. However, in cases where there are no for-loops at all, MATLAB Coder can fail to recognize when dynamic memory allocation is required.? Consider an example program that computes the Fibonacci sequence, where MATLAB Coder fails to produce an error. function y = myfib  ? ? persistent p p1; ? ? ? if isempty(p)  ? ? ? ? p = MyClass; p.count = 0;  ? ? ? ? p1 = MyClass; p1.count = 1;  ? ? end  ? ? p2 = p1;  ? ? p1 = p;  ? ? p = MyClass;  ? ? p.count = p1.count + p2.count;  ? ? y = p.count;  end  classdef MyClass < handle ? ?? ? properties  ? ? ? ? count ? ? ? end  end  When executing this function in MATLAB, you get:? >> myfib  1?  >> myfib?  1?  >> myfib?  2?  >> myfib?  3?  >> myfib?  5?  >> ...?  When you compile?myfib ?to a MEX function using codegen, and execute the MEX function, you get incorrect answers:? >> codegen myfib  >> myfib_mex?  1?  >> myfib_mex?  1?  >> myfib_mex?  2?  >> myfib_mex?  4?  >> ...?  This program requires dynamic memory allocation (with garbage collection) to properly function. The program has no explicit for-loops. There is an implicit for-loop because you can call the top-level function an unknown number of times. MATLAB Coder is unable to detect this contingency and fails to issue an error.

Summary: Dialog boxes of bus blocks inserted using quick insert can show empty signal hierarchy?
Description: If you use the quick insert functionality to create a Bus Selector, Bus Assignment, or Bus Creator block, the block parameters dialog box can show an empty signal hierarchy, even though the block is connected to a bus signal. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code does not contain code for Switch block input that does not route to output
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: A non-tunable constant value feeds into the Control input signal  parameter of a Switch block. The Threshold  parameter of the Switch block contains a non-tunable value.  Due to the above conditions, at all time steps, the same switchsignal connects to the output. As a result, the code generator might omit the code forthe block feeding into the data input port of the Switch block that does notroute to the output. Omitting this code is incorrect when the signal that feedsinto the data input port of the Switch block that does not route to the outputalso performs an operation such as writing to a Data Store Memory block or a MATLABFunction persistent variable.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Leaf state activity output from an atomic subchart does not get reset when the subchart becomes inactive
Description: If an atomic subchart has leaf state activity output enabled, the output port is not reset to None  when the atomic subchart becomes inactive.
Workaround: Wrap the contents of the atomic subchart in a new state that can serve as a container.Configure the container state to output its leaf state activity.Change the name of the output variable and its enumerated type to match that of the atomic subchart's leaf state activity output.Turn off leaf state activity output on the atomic subchart.Verify that the container state's leaf state activity output is mapped correctly to the parent chart's output.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect numerical result or crash due to missing data alignment directives for exported or GetSet custom storage class
Description: Generated model code might produce incorrect numerical results or crash if?all of the following conditions are true: The?compiler for the target processor compiled code that?produces?numeric results that differ from simulation results or crashes when using an instruction that requires specific data alignments?and?the data is not aligned properly. During code generation, a code replacement occurs that requires arguments to be aligned. An argument that requires data alignment is associated with one of the following: A built-in custom storage class with DataScope  set to Exported . For example, the argument references a Simulink data object for which the Storage class  (CustomStorageClass ) attribute is set to ExportToFile . Imported built-in custom storage class GetSet .    Under these conditions, code generation fails to emit data alignment directives for the argument and execution produces incorrect numerical results or crashes. Built-in storage classes ExportedGlobal  and SimulinkGlobal  are not affected by this bug.

Summary: Deleting a For-Each Subsystem block during mask initialization of another block crashes Simulink
Description: If you try to delete a For-Each Subsystem from the mask initialization script of its parent masked subsystem, Simulink crashes when you do Update Diagram (Ctrl+D) on the model. For example, suppose the mask initialization script of a masked parent subsystem includes the following code: if strcmp(get_param(gcs,'SimulationStatus'),'updating')  delete_block([gcs, '/ForEachSubsystem' ])  end    This causes Simulink to crash when you do Update Diagram on the model.
Workaround: Contact MathWorks support if this issue impacts you. Please create a Service Request at: https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.htmlWhen you create a Service Request, please provide your license number, MATLAB version, and operating system. You can get this information by typing 'ver' at the command prompt. Technical Support requests require that you are enrolled in our Software Maintenance Service.

Summary: Assertion in fixed-point models with coverage when simulating a model containing a RelationalOperator block with boolean inputs
Description: MATLAB might crash and show a stack trace ending with the following: MATLAB: number/numberbodyfactory.cpp:58: : Assertion `Unknown type of number requested!' failed.
Workaround: Turn off the relational boundary metric in coverage settings. Open in model Analysis>Coverage Settings and check off Relational Boundary in the Metrics sections.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Trigger-enabled Stateflow charts with nonzero initialized local variables and registered outputs can cause simulation mismatch in generated HDL code
Description: When generating HDL code from Stateflow charts, the code generator creates clock-driven registers for the following cases: Local variables that are read from before being written into (RAW) in any of the states ?The Initialize Outputs Every Time Chart Wakes Up  check box?is not selected for Mealy Charts and the output is not written into in any of the states.  In such cases, if the initial values for these registered local variables or outputs are non-zero in a trigger-enabled Stateflow block, then it might cause a numeric mismatch during simulation of the generated HDL code. The mismatch is observed in the initial cycles of the HDL simulation.  The mismatch occurs because trigger-enabled Stateflow Charts are guarded by a bypass register whose initial value is zero until the trigger event rising edge is high.
Workaround: The first few initial cycles of mismatch can be ignored by specifying the following parameter:???>> hdlset_param('<DUT-name-path>', 'IgnoreDataChecking', <ignore-initial-cycle-count-number>);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation produces a different result than MATLAB? for A(X) = [] when X is an empty matrix
Description: When X  is an empty matrix, code generation produces a different result than MATLAB? for the following code: A(X) = [] ; In MATLAB, when X  is not empty, A  becomes a row vector. When X  is empty, A  does not change. In code generation, A  always becomes a row vector even when X  is empty. This code generation behavior applies to: MEX, libraries and executables generated by MATLAB?  Coder? MATLAB Function block simulation and code generation MATLAB System block accelerated simulation and code generation.    ? ?
Workaround: Before you generate standalone C/C++ code,  to detect differences between MATLAB? and generated code:Using MATLAB?Coder?:               1.  Generate a MEX function.              2.  Compare the results of the MEX function with the results from MATLAB.Using the MATLAB Function block in a Simulink model, simulate the model.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Image Statistics simulation errors when using floating-point input, fixed-point input, or images larger than 643 pixels. HDL code generation from Image Statistics errors if the "mean" output port is disabled.
Description: This patch addresses four issues: (1) In Simulink?, when the pixel input is a floating-point data type, the Image Statistics block returns a compilation error. Invalid format. Problem creating simulation target MEX-file for model XXXX. Index expression out of bounds. Attempted to access element 1. The valid range is 1-0. In MATLAB?, when the pixel input is a floating-point data type, the visionhdl.ImageStatistics System object? fails to return validOut=true. The object returns the correct data, but without the validOut qualifier. (2) In Simulink, when the pixel input is a fixed-point data type, the Image Statistics block returns a compilation error. MATLAB System block 'modelName/Image Statistics' error occurred when invoking 'getOutputDataTypeImpl' Caused by: Conversion to char from embedded.numerictype is not possible. In MATLAB, the visionhdl.ImageStatistics System object simulates fixed-point input correctly. (3) The Image Statistics block (or System object) returns a run-time error when there are greater than 643  pixels between vStart and vEnd. A runtime error occurred in the System object 'visionhdl.ImageStatistics' in MATLAB System block 'modelName/Image Statistics', while executing the 'stepImpl' method. Index exceeds matrix dimensions. Index value # exceeds valid range [1-64]. The intended behavior is that the block calculates statistics on the first 643  pixels, and returns validOut=true at that time. It then waits for a vEnd and subsequent vStart to restart calculations on the next frame. (4) The Image Statistics block (or System object) returns an error from HDL code generation if you disable the "mean" output port. Error:Evaluation of elaborate function on class visionhdlsupport.ImageStatistics failed with the error message: MATLAB:minrhs: Not enough input arguments.

Summary: Status of proof objectives reported too conservatively for models containing lookup tables in property proving analysis
Description: For models containing lookup tables and proof objectives dependent on the lookup tables, property proving analysis in some cases may conservatively report the objective status as 'undecided due to stubbing,' i.e. 'proven valid' or 'falsified' even though it is decided.

Summary: Scope display titles cannot show unicode strings
Description: You can specify a value for the title using the Title  property in the axes properties dialog box. If the value specified has unicode characters (for instance, a string containing Japanese characters), the title does not render on the Scope display although it appears in the dialog.
Workaround: For Scope blocks only: In R2014b, use the replace_scopeblk('model name') script to replace the old Scopes with the new Scopes. In R2015a, use the "Try Time Scope" button to replace the old Scopes with the new Scopes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Type Conversion block with boolean output data type and 'Saturate on integer overflow' selected may produce incorrect generated code

The 'Saturate on integer overflow' parameter is selected. The 'Integer rounding mode' for the block is set to either 'Simplest', 'Floor', 'Zero', or 'Ceiling'.
Workaround: To work around this bug, clear the 'Saturate on integer overflow' parameter of the Data Type Conversion block.

Summary: Opening a block diagram test harness throws a warning if the name of the referenced Simulink model in the test harness is changed on disk

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Interpolation Using Prelookup block may result in incorrect generated code
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block may result in incorrect generated code when all the following are true: Integer rounding mode  for the block is set to Convergent . Saturate on integer overflow  is enabled for the block. The data types of both Table data  and Intermediate results  contain a slope that is not a power of two. In the Configuration Parameters > Hardware Implementation  pane, the number of bits for long  or long long  is larger than the number of bits for int .  Attached is a MATLAB script detectInterpBlkCodeGenIssue_1223756  which may help detect instances of this bug. NOTE: This script requires a Fixed-Point Designer license. This script is only meant as a diagnostic tool.  To use this script: Download files detectInterpBlkCodeGenIssue_1223756.m  and detectInterpBlkCodeGenIssue_1223756.p  into your current working directory, or a directory on the MATLAB path. At the MATLAB command prompt, enter the following command with a valid Simulink model name:  ret =  detectInterpBlkCodeGenIssue_1223756(model)  This script will look at the input model and returns a vector of block handles for the blocks which are susceptible to this bug. ?
Workaround: Perform any of the following to workaround this bug:Change the rounding mode on the block to a mode other than Convergent.Disable Saturate on integer overflow for the block.In R2014b and R2015a, in the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Code Style pane, set Casting modes to Explicit.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Incorrect connection of clock enable signal in generated HDL implementation of some Vision HDL Toolbox blocks and System objects operating on kernel sizes [2x2],[2x3],[3x2] and [3x3].
Description: An issue in the HDL implementation of algorithms operating on [2x2],[2x3],[3x2] and [3x3] neighborhoods causes the last active pixel on each line to be discarded. You may encounter this issue when: using FPGA-in-the-Loop. The auto-generated model reports the mismatch between the FPGA and Simulink output images. running the generated HDL on hardware or in an HDL simulator, if you toggle the clock enable signal. (i.e. the block is part of a multi-rate system and not running at the highest rate)  This affects eight kernel-based blocks (and the corresponding System objects) shipped in Vision HDL Toolbox version 1.0: Demosaic Interpolator Edge Detector Image Filter Median Filter Closing Dilation Erosion Opening  For Image Filter and Morphological Operations (Opening, Closing, Erosion, Dilation), kernel size corresponds to the size of the neighborhood. The mismatch only occurs when you set the kernel size to 2x2, 3x3, 2x3 or 3x2. Other kernel sizes are not affected. Demosaic Interpolator experiences the issue when you select 'Bilinear' algorithm. Edge Detector experiences the issue for all selectable algorithms. Median Filter experiences the issue when you set the 'Neighborhood size' to 3x3.

Summary: Dead Logic Detection inconsistency when generating tests for models that exhibit run-time errors
Description: Generating tests for models that exhibit run-time errors may satisfy objectives that are identified as dead by Dead Logic Detection.
Workaround: Identify all Integer Overflow, Division-by-Zero, and Out of Bound Array Access errors in the model and fix them before performing Dead Logic Detection or generating tests.

Summary: Error when generating test cases for models with inputs of certain enumeration types
Description: Simulink Design Verifier throws an error when generating test cases for a model with inputs of custom-defined enumeration data types. Cannot find a member of the 'ENUM_CLASS_NAME' enumeration class that corresponds to each element of the given input argument. where ENUM_CLASS_NAME  is the name of the class which defines the enum. This occurs when the enumeration type is defined as a subclass of one of the following built-in data types: int8 , uint8 , int16 , uint16 , or int32 .

Summary: Root inports pass C++ reference argumentsto subsystem functions by value instead of by reference
Description: When the C++ class interface configuration uses the I/O arguments step method , the root inportsthat are configured to pass data by Reference  to subsystem functionsinstead pass data by Value . This issue causes extra data copies of the referencearguments for the inports to occur.
Workaround: In the Configure C++ class interface dialogbox, change the Category for the root inport from Reference to Pointer.

Summary: The fread function with udp interface objects might return incomplete data
Description: Reading data using the fread  function with udp  interface objects can intermittently result in incomplete data being returned, and the following warning message is displayed before specified Timeout  duration expires: "Warning: Unsuccessful read: The specified amount of data was not returned within the Timeout period."  ?

Summary: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for some constant vectors replaced by Bus Assignment block
Description: Simulink Code Inspector returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if both of the following conditions exist: The Constant value  parameter of a Constant block is a vector that contains identical values. A Bus Assignment block replaces this constant vector element with a different value.

Summary: SIL or PIL simulation of AUTOSAR model fails when Simulink function name does not match corresponding client-server operation name
Description: In an AUTOSAR client-server model, an AUTOSAR operation name can differ from the corresponding Simulink function name. However, if the names differ, running a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation on the model fails with an error message that contains the text 'could not find entry for "WriteBlock" in binfo '.

Summary: Test harness created for a model fails to simulate in some cases
Description: Simulink throws an error when you simulate a test harness that meets all of the following conditions: The test harness was created for the model The test harness was modified prior to starting the simulation  The error thrown is similar to the following: Can not update the model reference target of 'myModel' used in 'myModel_Harness1' because it has unsaved changesError while bringing model references of model 'myModel_Harness1' up to date:  see errors reported above for details.
Workaround: Save the model before starting the simulation.

Summary: Clicking the perspectives control button in a model after viewing harness models with Dashboard blocks crashes MATLAB
Description: Executing the following actions will crash MATLAB: Click on the perspectives control on the lower-right corner of a model to view a harness model that contains Dashboard blocks. Click on the perspectives control in the harness model to return to the original model. Click on the perspectives control again in the original model.  ?

Description: Simulating or updating a model containing an atomic subchart with mapped events can cause this error message: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. Summary: Viewing the contents of a MAT-file in the Current Folder browser details panel may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you view the contents of a MAT-file containing one or more Simulink objects in the details panel of the Current Folder browser without Simulink running, Simulink attempts to start, and possibly crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: If you know that a MAT-file contains Simulink objects, avoid selecting it for preview until Simulink has loaded.

Summary: Zero-crossing data structures generated for a model containing Variant model blocks may be empty
Description: When a model containing variant model blocks is configured to generate preprocessor conditionals, the compiled zero-crossing data structure in the code generated from the model may be empty under certain preprocessor conditions. For example, if a model contained a Variant Model block with two variants, and only the first submodel had a zero-crossing detection, then the zero-crossing data structure for that top model is similar to the following:  typedef struct {#if S1  rtZCE_bot1 Model_1_DWORK1;#endif} PrevZCSigStates_top; If the second variant is the one to be compiled into the executable, so that S1  was false when compiling the code, the structure PrevZCSigStates_top  has no elements.
Workaround: If one of the submodels does not introduce an element into the zero-crossing data structure, add a block to the submodel that results in the creation of a zero-crossing element for that submodel. For example, add a Triggered Subsystem block to a submodel to introduce an element corresponding to the submodel into the top model's zero-crossing data structure.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible?wrong answer?in generated code for Interpolation using Prelookup block related to output data type overflow
Description: Incorrect?code may be generated from the Interpolation using Prelookup block?when these?conditions?are all true: The Saturate on integer overflow  check box is selected  The Interpolation method  is set to?Linear  Table Data Type ,?Intermediate results Data Type ,?and?Output Data Type ?are fixed-point data types Table Data Type  and?Output Data Type  have the same slope adjustment factor and bias Table Data Type ?does not overflow if cast to?Intermediate results Data type  or the?Output Data Type   Intermediate results Data type ?may overflow if cast to?Output Data Type  The?Integer Rounding mode ?is set to either:Ceiling ,?Convergent ,?Round , or?Nearest ?when?the?Table data ?Value is close to the maximum value of the data type Floor ,?Simplest , or?Zero ?when the?Table data ?Value is close to the minimum value of the data type    Computation of the interpolation overflows the?Intermediate results ?Data Type   If these conditions are all present, a wrapping effect may occur instead of a saturation effect in the output of the Interpolation using Prelookup block.
Workaround: Set the??Output data type?parameter of the Interpolation Using Prelookup block to?the same data type as the?Intermediate results data typeAdd and connect a?Data Type Conversion block to the output of the Interpolation Using Prelookup blockSelect the Saturate on integer overflow check box of the Data Type Conversion blockSet the?Output data type?parameter of the?Data Type Conversion block to the desired data type

Summary: Slow packaging process when deploying MATLAB code
Description: When using mcc  or deploytool  to package MATLAB code for deployment, the packaging process is two or three times slower than it was in R2014b for the same code. The MATLAB command matlab.codetools.requiredFilesAndProducts  is also affected by the slow down.
Workaround: Workaround 1Run MATLAB or the mcc or deploytool commands as administrator.Workaround 2Install MATLAB in a location where the user can write to without administrator privileges.Workaround 3Give full read/write permissions to the user for the dependency analysis database files located at:matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\depfun\+matlab\+depfun\+internal\requirements_arch_dfdbmatlabroot\toolbox\matlab\depfun\+matlab\+depfun\+internal\pcm_arch_dbwhere arch is the platform architecture, such as win64, and matlabroot is the MATLAB installation location, such as:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015aChanging the user permissions may require administrator privileges once.  Any compilation after that should not need it.

Summary: Error when setting a breakpoint in some MATLAB Function blocks that use fixed-point values
Description: When you set a breakpoint on any line of a MATLAB Function block that has both the Allow direct feedthrough  option turned off and uses fixed-point values, Stateflow throws an error when you try to simulate the model: Error using autobuild_kernel>autobuild_local (line 194)Unable to inline 'eML_blk_kernel'. It might be due to recursive calls.

Summary: Setting the TimeZone property of an unzoned datetime array to 'UTCLeapSeconds' returns incorrect result
Description: When converting an unzoned datetime array to 'UTCLeapSeconds'  by setting the TimeZone  property, additional seconds are incorrectly added to the times. For example, the following code should return 2012-06-30T00:00:00.000Z : >> t1 = datetime(2012,6,30,0,0,0)t1 =  30-Jun-2012 00:00:00>> t1.TimeZone = 'UTCLeapSeconds't1 =  2012-06-30T00:00:24.000Z   ?
Workaround: To convert an unzoned datetime array to 'UTCLeapSeconds', use the datevec function and create a new datetime array:>> t1 = datetime(2012,6,30,0,0,0)t1 =  30-Jun-2012 00:00:00>> t1 = datetime(datevec(t1),'TimeZone','UTCLeapSeconds')t1 =  2012-06-30T00:00:00.000ZThis is not necessary when converting from unzoned to any other time zone, or when converting any other time zone to/from 'UTCLeapSeconds'. In those cases, simply set the TimeZone property. For example:>> t1 = datetime(2012,6,30,0,0,0)t1 =  30-Jun-2012 00:00:00>> t1.TimeZone = 'America/New_York't1 =  30-Jun-2012 00:00:00>> t1.Format = 'dd-MMM-yyyy HH:mm:ss z't1 =    30-Jun-2012 00:00:00 EDT?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening dialog for bus routing blocks in Simulink
Description: While modifying models using the Simulink editor, users might experience crashes when attempting to open the dialog for bus routing blocks. Bus routing blocks include Bus Creator, Bus Selector, and Bus Assignment.
Workaround: Run the attached MATLAB script BR1220118.p prior to opening the dialog for any offending bus routing blocks. The attached function will only need to be run once per MATLAB session. You can add this script to a startup file (startup.m or matlabrc.m) to run automatically upon MATLAB startup.This script will alter the behavior of the Find button functionality within the dialogs for any bus routing blocks. Use the Highlight Signal to Source right-click option for the signals feeding into the bus routing blocks as a workaround.

Summary: Error when generating tests for subsystems containing enabled or triggered model reference blocks
Description: Generating tests for subsystems that contain Enabled or Triggered model reference blocks will error out. The following error will be thrown: Invalid Simulink object name:

Summary: Configset reference in data dictionary causes slowdown opening Simulink models.
Description: If a Simulink model uses Configset reference and a data dictionary (SLDD file) then there is a significant slowdown in loading the model.
Workaround: Do not use Configset reference and data dictionary (SLDD file) together.

Summary: Unable use rapid accelerator mode to simulate a model that uses the duration operator in a Test Sequence block
Description: If you use the duration  operator in a Test Sequence block, and try to simulate the model in rapid accelerator mode, the following error message is displayed: Code generation and simulation targets registered different sets of persistent variables for the block. External or Rapid Accelerator mode simulation requires code generation and simulation targets to register the same set of persistent variables for this block. This discrepancy is typically caused by MATLAB functions that have different code paths for simulation and code generation targets where these code paths define different sets of persistent variables. Please identify these code paths in the offending block and rewrite the MATLAB code so that the set of persistent variables is the same between simulation and code generation.
Workaround: You may be able to work around this issue by turning off debugging: >> sfc coder_options forceDebugOff 1Note that this turns off animation and debugging for all test sequence blocks and any Stateflow charts in the current MATLAB session.This will also prevent you from setting breakpoints, which could complicate debugging.

Summary: Converting blocks including a Bus Creator block into a subsystem can cause model update or simulation error
Description: If you select a group of blocks in the model and convert the blocks to a subsystem, using either the right-click menu option or the marquee selection action, an error can occur later during model update or simulation if: The set of blocks being converted includes a Bus Creator block. That Bus Creator block is connected directly to one of the subsystem inputs. In other words, there is no intervening block between an input to the converted subsystem and the Block Creator block.  This is the error message that Simulink generates: Selected signal 'SignalName' in the BlockType block 'BlockPath' cannot be found in the input bus signal.
Workaround: Use either of these workarounds:Prior to the creation of the subsystem, disconnect the signals connected to the Bus Creator block. After the conversion, reconnect the signals to the subsystem input ports.After the creation of the subsystem, in the Signal Properties dialog box for each signal reported in the error message, enable the Show propagated signals parameter. To restore the model to its original configuration, you can disable the Show propagated signals parameter for each signal that you just enabled.

Summary: Optimal objective value fval returned by intlinprog and the linprog 'dual-simplex' algorithm may not match f' * x.
Description: When intlinprog  (R2014a, R2014b and R2015a) or the linprog  'dual-simplex'  algorithm (R2014b and R2015a) solve a problem with the LPPreprocess  (intlinprog ) or Preprocess  (linprog  'dual-simplex' ) option set to its default value, the optimal objective value fval  may not match f' * x  where f  is the objective vector and x  is the optimal solution.
Workaround: Compute f' * x to get the correct optimal objective value or disable LP preprocessing by setting the LPPreprocess (intlinprog) or Preprocess (linprog 'dual-simplex') option to 'none' using optimoptions.

Summary: Using a deleted data dictionary variable with fast restart does not error out during simulation
Description: If a data dictionary variable used in a block parameter is deleted and the simulation is using fast restart, the first simulation shows an error about the deleted variable. However, subsequent simulations will not show an error and will use the previously evaluated value.

Summary: Incorrect sample time setting for function-call generator in test harness
Description: If you create a test harness for a periodic function-call subsystem, the function-call generator sample time can be set incorrectly. This happens when both of the following are true: The function-call subsystem has a sample time specified, and is not equal to 1 The synthesized function-call generator is not updated to reflect the current sample time.   The default setting for the function-call generator is 1, which does not match the trigger port setting.
Workaround: Delete the sample time setting in the function-call generator block and replace it with -1. This allows Simulink to compute an appropriate periodic rate to use with the block.

Summary: Opening Simulink Perspectives can hang MATLAB if the model includes blocks from the Dashboard library
Description: Using the Perspectives button in a model that contains Dashboard blocks can hang MATLAB, with no way of breaking out.

Summary: The kernel does not reset the overload counters for multirate models.
Description: The kernel does not clear the overload counters when multirate models start to run. As a result, the models falsely report larger overload counter values. This bug does not affect single-rate models.
Workaround: You have three options:1. Use the Set Overload counter block to reset the overload counter in the model.2. Contact technical support to get a R2014b patch.3. Upgrade to the R2015b release.?

Summary: Unable to deploy the serial command with MATLAB Compiler
Description: If the MATLAB serial  command is deployed, the resulting component may fail with the following error during runtime: Error using serial (line 120)Cannot create: No class com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.SerialComm can be located on the Java class pathError in testSerial (line 2)MATLAB:serial:serial:cannotCreate
Workaround: Create a file named javaclasspath.txt in your MATLAB preferences directory.  The preferences directory can be found with the prefdir command in MATLAB.In the javaclasspath.txt file, add the following two lines:$matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/instrument.jar$matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/testmeas.jarThe $matlabroot text should be kept as is, do not change it to a specific path.  Restart MATLAB and recompile the component.

Summary: Incorrect linearization Jacobian matrix for Bus Assignment block
Description: For an Bus Assignment block that has a nonvirtual bus input, the linearize  function and the Linear Analysis > Linearize Block  menu item each produce an incorrect Jacobian matrix.
Workaround: Use one of these approaches:Change the nonvirtual input bus to a virtual input bus.Insert a Signal Conversion block before Bus Assignment block to convert the nonvirtual input bus to a virtual bus.

Summary: Dragging blocks from library browser to model can cause crash
Description: MATLAB can crash when you drag blocks from the Library Brower into a model, particularly when a large library needs to load for the first time or when other loads cause delays during the drag.
Workaround: Rather than dragging blocks to a model, you can:Add blocks using the Quick Insert feature added in R2014bRight-click the block in the library, and select Add block to model modelname (Ctrl-I)Open the library from the library browser and then copy and paste the blocks into your model

Summary: Code generation error for models with masked reusable subsystems when Inline parameters is cleared
Description: Code generation displays an error when a model meets all of the following conditions: The model configuration parameter Inline parameters  is cleared. The model contains multiple instances of a masked subsystem, which are identical except for the values used to specify the mask parameters. The subsystem has the Function packaging  parameter, on the Code Generation  tab of the subsystem parameters dialog box, set to Auto  or Reusable function . The subsystem has more than one mask parameter. Multiple blocks in the subsystem use one of the mask parameters in an inconsistent way. For example, one block in the subsystem uses the mask parameter with a particular numeric data type, while another block uses the same mask parameter with a different data type.  The error appears similar to this example: Code generation assertion 'static_cast<int>(ad->getIndex()) == argDefIdx' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\simulink\sl_engin\rtwcg_context_impl.cpp:5252'.

Summary: A sequence of datetimes created with the colon operator, using a mixed-unit calendar duration step size, gives the wrong answer
Description: A datetime sequence created using the colon operator, for example startTime:step:stopTime , is defined as seq = [startTime startTime+step (startTime+step)+step ...]  However, when the step size is a calendar duration that contains mixed units, the actual result may not be equal to that definition. For example, the following code returns an incorrect result: >> startTime = datetime('1-Jan-2015');>> step = calmonths(1) + caldays(28);>> stopTime = datetime('1-May-2015');>> seq = startTime:step:stopTimeseq =   01-Jan-2015 01-Mar-2015 26-Apr-2015  This is equal to [startTime startTime+step startTime+2*step] . Because calendar arithmetic is not associative, this is not equal to the definition above, and the correct result is 01-Jan-2015 01-Mar-2015 29-Apr-2015  ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model Reference block inside a For Each subsystem may produce incorrect generated code
Description: When a Model Reference block is put inside a For Each subsystem, it is possible that the ForEach  induction variable is used as an array index after the ForEach  loop is processed, resulting in incorrect code. As an example, extract the attached Example.zip and build the model, named top.slx . Note how the variable named forEach_itr_i32  is used as an array index inside the ForEach  loop and then again afterward in expressions for the output ports. ?

Summary: Error during parallel builds for referenced models
Description: Building a model reference hierarchy may result in an error when the following conditions exist: You are using a parallel pool of workers You are simulating a model that contains models referenced in Accelerator mode, or generating code for a model hierarchy A referenced model loads other models when it is loaded, e.g., using pre- or post-load callbacks  MATLAB displays the following error message under these conditions: Dimensions of matrices being concatenated are not consistent.
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1216312 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Error running the Import Tool's generated function code when importing a numeric matrix from a spreadsheet
Description: The function code generated by the Import Tool when you import a numeric matrix from a spreadsheet may error when run. This occurs if the spreadsheet selection contains all numeric values, and no rules have been applied.
Workaround: Import the spreadsheet selection as Column vectors or as a Table, rather than a Numeric Matrix. Or, use the readtable function to read in the spreadsheet file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect value for 0th element of static constant boolean array in generated code
Description: Incorrect code is possible for a model design that results in generated code that uses?a boolean constant array or matrix in any of the following contexts: The boolean constant contains both false and true values. This issue does not affect boolean constants in which every element has the same value. The boolean constant is used to compute an element-wise logical OR.  The boolean constant is used to compute an element-wise logical AND.  The boolean constant is used in?the part of an if  statement that evaluates to true or false. The boolean constant is used in the part?of a ternary operator (?: ) that evaluates to true or false. The boolean constant is the control input?to a Switch block.  The attached model issue_example.zip  contains examples of this issue with element-wise logical OR , element-wise logical AND , and a switch input signal. In the generated code this constant has an incorrect value for the 0th element: /* Output and update for atomic system: '<Root>/use_const_OR_ffttft' */  void issue_example_1214588_use_const_OR_ffttft(void)  {  ? static const boolean_T tmp[6] = { true, false, true, true, false, true };   ?
Workaround: Utilize a numeric type other than boolean to represent the array constant.

Summary: Package a Toolbox dialog box incorrectly reports additional MathWorks products as required
Description: When you package a toolbox, MATLAB analyzes your toolbox files to determine which MathWorks products are required. This list can contain extra products that the toolbox does not actually need.

Summary: Incorrect error reported during simulation of a model with a Vector Concatenate block driving a Merge block
Description: An error might be incorrectly reported when simulating a model if all  of the following conditions are true: The model has a Vector Concatenate block driving a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is connected to another Merge block. The branch containing the Vector Concatenate block is executing at some point during the simulation and its output is fed to the Merge block. The model is simulated using the sim  command or the Simulink Debugger.  Under these conditions, simulation might incorrectly report an error similar to the following: Output of Merge block 'modelname/subsystem/Merge' is not being updated at t = 0 because none of the blocks connected to the input ports of the Merge block are active. Ensure that the Merge block output is updated by one of its input signals at every time step
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block or a Gain block with a gain of 1 at the output of the Vector Concatenate block driving the Merge block.

Summary: To Workspace blocks with duplicate names crashes in external mode
Description: If  a model has multiple To Workspace blocks with the same name, and you set the simulation mode to External , Simulink crashes when you connect your model to a waiting or running target program.  You can connect your model to a target using one of these approaches: In the Simulink Editor, select Simulation > Connect To Target . In the Simulink Editor, with External  simulation mode selected, in the toolbar press the Connect to Target  button. In the External Mode Control panel, press the Connect  button.  ?
Workaround: Simulate the model in Normal mode with Stop Time set to 0.Fix the Duplicate data logging variable name error.Change the simulation mode to External.

Summary: Syntax-highlighted MATLAB code does not appear when the text component is under a subsection for some file formats
Description: When you use either the Word (from template) or HTML (from template) file formats, and have a Text component with the Show text as syntax-highlighted MATLAB code  option checked, if you place that component under any subsection, the text content will not appear in the generated report.
Workaround: Either place the Text component under a top-level Chapter only, or use a different file format.

Summary: MATLAB can produce unexpected results when training a pattern recognition network using the cross-entropy performance function
Description: MATLAB can produce results that are unexpected when the following are true: Training a pattern recognition network, usually created using patternnet  The performance function is using the default "crossentropy" value The target data has only one row   Below is an example when this behavior occurs, [x, t] = my_example_data(); % t has one row net = patternnet(); net = train( net, x, t ); y = net(x); where my_example_data  is an custom function that generates training data t  that has one row. This behavior occurs because the cross-entropy expression in the patternnet function expects the target data t  to have multiple rows with one row for each class, also known as 1-of-N encoding, instead binary encoding using a single row altogether.
Workaround: You can transform the single-row binary encoding to the multiple-rows 1-of-N encoding. Using the example above, this transformation can be done as follows:[x, t] = my_example_data(); % t has one row net = patternnet(); t = [t; ~t];net = train( net, x, t );y = net(x);Note that this workaround may give different results than those obtained in earlier MATLAB releases because of the different number of neurons in the output layer.

Summary: Using the Signal Builder block to import MAT-files that contain non-double data types displays errors
Description: Using the Signal Builder block to import MAT-files that contain non-double data types displays the following error in the MATLAB Command Window:  Error using sigbldr.controllers.SBFileImportDialogController/handleApplyButtonCallback (line 233) While setting the 'YData' property of Line: Value must be a vector of numeric type
Workaround: Data imported to Signal Builder block must be of data type double. To resolve this issue, cast the data to double and save it to a MAT-file. Note, the Signal Builder block only outputs data of type double.

Summary: MATLAB Production Server ships with OpenSSL version 1.0.1g, which is vulnerable to the FREAK vulnerability
Description: The FREAK vulnerability is a method in which an attacker who is able to intercept network communication can forcibly downgrade the client-server communication. This could result in using a weaker encryption cipher than was intended. This vulnerability was fixed in OpenSSL 1.0.1k.
Workaround: To mitigate the FREAK vulnerability, obtain the appropriate OpenSSL 1.0.1k binaries for your hardware architecture and overwrite the existing OpenSSL binaries found under the MATLAB Production Server installation folder.The files to be replaced on different hardware architectures are as follows:On Linux, mps_install_root/bin/glnxa64/libssl.so mps_install_root/bin/glnxa64/libcrypto.so On Mac, mps_install_root/bin/maci64/libssl.dylib mps_install_root/bin/maci64/libcrypto.dylib On Windows 32-bit, mps_install_root\bin\win32\libeay32.dllmps_install_root\bin\win32\ssleay32.dllOn Windows 64-bit, mps_install_root\bin\win64\libeay32.dllmps_install_root\bin\win64\ssleay32.dll**Where mps_install_root is the MATLAB Production Server installation folder. For example: /usr/local/MATLAB/MATLAB_Production_Server/R2015a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when copying blocks from a Simulink model using a data dictionary
Description: When copying blocks from one Simulink model to another, MATLAB will crash if the following conditions are true:  The model that the blocks are coming from is using a data dictionary that contains enumerated types.  There are no data dictionaries open in the Model Explorer or the MATLAB command window.
Workaround: Keep any data dictionary open while copying the blocks.

Summary: Code generation within the MATLAB Coder App may appear to fail with an error implicating flattenForJava.
Description: When using the MATLAB Coder App, the GUI may appear to fail and issue an error occurring in flattenForJava . For the error to occur, your MATLAB source code must use MATLAB objects or system objects that do no have any public, non-hidden properties. Within the GUI, this may be encountered on either the "Check for Run-Time Issues" step or the "Generate Code" step of the workflow.
Workaround: This error will not prevent the successful generation of code and other artifacts. The project itself will still be valid. In this sense, the error may be safely ignored.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Switch or Multiport Switch block in a multi-instance reusable subsystem can generate wrong code
Description: Your Simulink model can generate wrong code on building the model, if it meets all the following criteria: Your model has two or more identical atomic subsystems having a Switch or Multiport Switch block inside the subsystem. The control port of these blocks are driven by Constant blocks outside the subsystem. The values of the Constant block are such that they allow the Switch or Multiport Switch  block to select one input in one atomic subsystem and another input in another atomic subsystem.  On building this model, the generated code for the block does not have a conditional statement to handle the different inputs from the Constant block outside the subsystem. Instead, it is incorrectly optimized to take the input that is derived from the control port value of the instance of this subsystem that executes first. Hence, other instances may get the incorrect output from their Switch or Multiport Switch block.
Workaround: To work around this problem, clear the Inline Parameters check box in Model Configuration Parameters->Optimization->Signals and Parameters->Simulation and Code Generation and rebuild your model.Alternatively, you can add a Signal Conversion block after the Constant block.

Summary: Deployed component crashes if the brush tool is used
Description: If MATLAB code that utilizes the brush tool is deployed, the deployed component crashes when the user right clicks on the mouse after the points are selected with the brush tool. For example, if the following code is deployed: plot([1 2 3],[1 2 3],'-*');brush on; and then the user uses the brush tool during runtime and right clicks on the figure, the component crashes with a stack trace showing mcr!mcrInstance::MCallbackAttach
Workaround: Use the waitfor MATLAB command to prevent the MATLAB code from exiting while the figure is open.  This will allow the brush tool to operate without crashing the deployed component.For example, deploying the following code works:plot([1 2 3],[1 2 3],'-*');brush on;waitfor(gcf);

Summary: Dashboard blocks lose settings and connection in Simulink Test harnesses
Description: This bug affects two related workflows involving Dashboard blocks inside a Simulink Test harness. If a test harness is created from a subsystem that contains a connected Dashboard block, the Dashboard block loses its connection when you close the test harness. When the test harness is closed, the Dashboard block shows the Double-click to connect  message indicating that the Dashboard block is not connected. If a Dashboard block is dropped inside a harness, any change in the block settings or connections are lost when the harness is closed and reopened. If you close the model, reopen the model, and open the test harness, then the Dashboard block shows the Double-click to connect  message indicating that it is not connected.

Summary: Invalid Simulink object handle error when generating tests for Stateflow charts or atomic subcharts containing model references
Description: Simulink Design Verifier displays the following error message when you generate tests for some Stateflow charts or atomic subcharts containing Simulink Functions with model reference blocks: Invalid Simulink object handle

Summary: Exporting a Simulink model that contains test harnesses to a previous Simulink version in MDL format silently discards harnesses and harness information
Description: If you export a Simulink model that contains one or more test harnesses to a previous version of Simulink in MDL format, the exported model generated by the export process does not contain the test harnesses or test harness information.  However, no warning is thrown to indicate that harnesses and harness information will not be saved. This occurs because test harnesses in Simulink Test are only supported in SLX format for R2015a (Simulink Test version 1.0). If you export a Simulink model that contains one or more test harnesses specifically to R2014b in SLX format, harnesses and harness information are not deleted even though it will not be possible to work with the harnesses in R2014b.

Summary: Code generation can fail for models with operator annotations enabled
Description: Generating code for a model with Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Comments  > Operator annotations  enabled can generate an error ?
Workaround: To avoid triggering this bug, disable operator annotations using one of these approaches:Clear the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Comments > Operator annotations parameter.At the MATLAB command line, enter:       set_param(bdroot, 'OperatorAnnotations', 'off');

Summary: gamuliobj throws an error when using nonlinear constraints and a hybrid function
Description: gamultiobj  throws an error and stops when: You include a hybrid function (by setting the HybridFcn  option to @fgoalattain ). You have a nonlinear constraint function that returns a column vector or [].  Typically, a nonlinear constraint returns []  when it has only inequality constraints or only equality constraints.  For example, this nonlinear constraint function triggers an error when used with a hybrid function: function [c,ceq] = nonlinear_constraints(x)c(1) = x(:,1).^2 + x(:,3).^2 - x(:,2);c(2) = (x(:,1) + x(:,2)).*(10 - x(:,3));c = c' % a column vector will error% No equality constraints (will error)ceq = []; ?
Workaround: Have your nonlinear constraint function return zeros(1,0) instead of [], or ensure that it returns row vectors. For example,function [c,ceq] = nonlinear_constraints(x)c(1) = x(:,1).^2 + x(:,3).^2 - x(:,2);c(2) = (x(:,1) + x(:,2)).*(10 - x(:,3));% No equality constraints (does not error)ceq = zeros(1,0);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for multiple calls to Rte_Read within a runnable for an?explicit read root inport branched to non-reusable subsystem
Description: The code generator produces?multiple calls?to an RTE read function (Rte_Read ) within a runnable if all of these conditions are true:? Model includes a root inport configured with AUTOSAR explicit read access. The root inport is branched;?it?connects to?at least?two reads within the same runnable boundary. One of the reads is within a non-reusable subsystem. That non-reusable subsystem is configured with a void-void interface   for code generation.  Under these conditions,?if the value returned by Rte_Read   changes?while the?runnable?executes, multiple reads can produce an?incorrect answer.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block that directly connects to the root Inport?block (before branching) and select the block parameter?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization setting for the newly added block.

Summary: Incorrect analysis results caused by specified minimum and maximum values triggering fixed point optimizations
Description: Specified minimum and maximum values specified on blocks with fixed-point type outputs can incorrectly trigger certain fixed-point model optimizations for the destination blocks that consume those fixed-point outputs. The affected optimizations include removal of saturation semantics on integer overflow and in rare cases, choosing a smaller word length for the fixed point type. This bug can occur only when all the following conditions hold:A fixed point operation takes only fixed point inputs One of the fixed point inputs to the operation has a specified minimum maximum value on its actual source.

Summary: The mlabelzero22pi function errors for meridian labels along edge of map frame
Description: When meridian labels are positioned at the northern or southern edge of the map frame, the mlabelzero22pi  function throws an error and does not update the labels. This is very often the case with a cylindrical, pseudo-cylindrical, or conic projection (such as Mercator, Robinson, or Equal Area Conic, for example), and is not applicable to an azimuthal projection (such as Stereographic). The meridian label latitude is controlled by the map axes MLabelParallel property; the problem occurs when its value equals one of the values of the MapLatLimit property.
Workaround: The updates provided in this workaround are for release R2014b and release R2015a.  Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Download the attached file, workaround_1209755-14b-through-15a_2015-03-04.zip, to a folder of your choosing.       On Windows double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button, and extract the contents to a folder of your choosing. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux use the unzip command.       Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txt.

Summary: DOCXSubDoc fails if the input path contains a backslash
Description: MATLAB displays the following error message when a document is closed that contains a DOCXSubDoc  object with a path containing backslashes. Error using mlreportgen.dom.Document/closeError closing document package: Could not successfully parse URI string.
Workaround: Replace all backslashes in the reference document path with slashes before calling DOCXSubDoc. For example:subDocPath = strrep(subDocPath, '\', '/');rpt = Document('Report', 'docx');open(rpt);para = append(rpt, mlreportgen.dom.Paragraph('About Us'));append(rpt, DOCXSubDoc(subDocPath));close(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);

Summary: Cannot find devices when Data Acquisition Toolbox code is deployed
Description: Data Acquisition Toolbox code, such as daq.getDevices  or daq.getVendors , fail to find devices when deployed, even when the devices are connected and properly configured. If you're using DirectSound, apply the workarounds in EBR 1205811  as well as this EBR.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you can do one of the following:Workaround 1Add the following line anywhere in your MATLAB code and recompile:%#function registerrealtimecataloglocationWorkaround 2If using mcc to compile your code, pass the-a registerrealtimecataloglocationoption to the mcc command.Workaround 3If using deploytool, add the registerrealtimecataloglocation mex function from the matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hwconnectinstaller directory to your deployment project as an additional required file.  matlabroot is your MATLAB installation directory, such as: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015a

Summary: MATLAB can crash when Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks do not check dynamic memory allocation success before first access
Description: Stateflow charts and MATLAB Function blocks use C dynamic memory allocation functions such as malloc and calloc to allocate space for instance variables for simulation. Simulink sometimes does not check the validity of the pointers returned by such functions before using them. If Simulink does not check the pointer validity, allocations can fail due to insufficient contiguous memory on the heap. This results in a crash. This occurs more commonly on 32-bit platforms. This bug affects only the simulation environment. This bug does not affect code generated by Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder.

Summary: MISRA-C:2004 Technical Corrigendum 1 not supported for rule 19.2
Description: MISRA-C:2004 Rule 19.2 says, "Nonstandard characters should not occur in header file names in #include directives." However, the MISRA-C:2004 Technical Corrigendum 1 says that the use of the \ character is permitted in filename paths.Polyspace does not support this technical corrigendum for rule 19.2. For example, the following statement should not raise a 19.2 violation.#include "folder1\abcd.h"  The same issue also occurs for \\ .  For example, the following statement incorrectly raises a 19.2 violation.#include ".\\folder1\\folder2  ?

Summary: "Element name mismatch" diagnostic does not work when set to error
Description: If you set the Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity > Element name mismatch  diagnopstic to error , then when a Bus Creator block passes a bus signal without bus object to an Inport block with a bus object specified, the diagnostic does not report the error even if the bus signal has different element names than the definition in the bus object.
Workaround: Specify the bus object at the Bus Creator block.

Summary: Unused function might be generated in export function-call subsystems or export function models
Description: An unused function can be generated for virtual subsystems containing more than one function-call subsystem whose outputs are connected to a Merge or Mux block. The functions are exported by right-clicking the virtual subsystem and choosing 'C/C++ Code ' > 'Export Functions '.  Starting in R2015a, an unused function can also be generated for referenced export function models under the same conditions.

Summary: Abnormal termination of MATLAB when working with multi-threaded .NET events
Description: Handling high frequency of .NET  events when calling .NET  libraries from MATLAB can cause a System.IndexOutOfRangeException  error, which can cause MATLAB to terminate abnormally.
