Summary: During update diagram, Stateflow chart containing graphical function generates error
Description: A Stateflow chart containing a graphical function can generate the following error stack trace during update diagram "failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\export\include\cg_ir\type\type.hpp at line 431:  T::isa((const CG::Type*)aType)" The error is triggered when a chart has these conditions: 1) Stateflow chart is using MATLAB as the action language. 2) Chart has a graphical function containing a "For Loop" pattern. 3) The loop index variable is not defined by a Stateflow data object.
Workaround: Define the loop index variable with a chart level data object of "Local" scope.

Summary: Performance regression for models that use parameter structures
Description: You can use the fields of structures to set block parameter values in a model. For example, you can use the field myParams.myGain  in a Gain block, and the field myParams.myOffset  in a Constant block. In R2015a, models that use parameter structures simulate more slowly than in previous releases.

Summary: UI components inside uipanel objects disappear in GUIDE
Description: When viewing a UI layout in GUIDE, the components inside a uipanel  disappear when the Visible  property of the uipanel  object is set to 'off' . However, MATAB? displays the contents of the uipanel  display at run-time.

Summary: Unable to open or save Simulink models in a drive mounted as a subfolder of another drive
Description: On Windows, if an external drive is mounted as a subfolder of another drive (i.e., a "volume mount point"), you cannot open or save Simulink models on the external drive. You see an error message similar to the following:  Error using open (line 162) fl:filesystem:PathNotFound   Error using open (line 162), No error message found    You see these problems when:Trying to open models on a mounted volume. Trying to save models when your tempdir is on a mounted volume. Trying to start Simulink when it is installed on a mounted volume.
Workaround: Mount the external drive with its own drive letter. Go to Disk Management, either via Computer Management or by running the command diskmgmt.msc.In the Disk Management window, right-click the external volume and choose Change Drive Letter and Paths. In the Change Drive Letter and Paths dialog box, choose Add. In the Add Drive Letter of Path dialog box, select Assign the following drive letter and choose an unused letter.

Summary: Parameter values in generated test cases might not match the dimension of the parameter object
Description: Test generation might give incorrect results if all of the following conditions are true:   You are using the parameter tuning workflow of Simulink Design Verifier  One of the tuned model parameters is described using a Simulink.Parameter object in the workspace  The tuned Simulink.Parameter object has a non-scalar dimension  Test generation will succeed, but the generated tests might have values whose dimensions do not match the Simulink.Parameter dimension. Creating a test harness and simulating the harness model will result in the error Size mismatch for MATLAB expression .

Summary: Incorrect unreachable checks in an unreachable block
Description: In your verification results, the Source  pane displays a gray code block, but when you click on an individual operation in the block, a different color appears on the Check Details  pane. This color mismatch occurs when the code block is in a function that you specify for the option Sensitivity context   (-context-sensitivity  on command line).  If this situation occurs, for each check in the block, on the Check Details  pane, click on the Show calling context for check  button. On a separate Sensitivity Context  pane, you can see the true check color for each invocation of the function containing the code block.

Summary: MATLAB crashes or returns incorrect values when accessing the CData property of a patch object
Description: MATLAB can return incorrect values when accessing the CData  property of an object that matches all the following: The patch object was created using any of the following functions: trisurf , fill , fill3 , pi , or patch . The Faces  property is defined using non-double datatype values.  For example, MATLAB may crash when executing the following code: vertices = [0 0;1 0;1 1;0 1]; faces = uint8([1 2 3 4]); faceVertexCData = [1 0 0;0 1 0;0 0 1;0 0 0];p = patch('Faces',faces,'Vertices',vertices,'FaceVertexCData',faceVertexCData ,'FaceColor','interp');cdata = p.CData;  MATLAB can also crash while adding a legend or colorbar to a figure containing the patch object.
Workaround: Convert your data to a double datatype prior to defining the Faces property of the patch object.

Summary: Error when exported function model has more than 50 root Inport blocks
Description: A model with a referenced export-function model causes an error on building when all  of the following conditions occur: The export-function model has a large number of root level Inport blocks (typically more than 50). The export-function model has more than one exported function. One or more of the Inport blocks with data outputs feed multiple exported functions.  The error is:  The number of columns specified did not match the actual number of columns in all of the rows.
Workaround: Use either of these approaches to fix the issue:Reduce the number of Inport blocks at the root level of the export-function model.Ensure that the root-level Inport blocks that output data signals within the export-function model only feed a single exported function.

Description: The HDL testbench generated by HDL Coder can give a false pass test result because of the following issues:  When the value of IgnoreDataChecking  is greater than 0 clock cycles, the HDL testbench silently does not validate the results of the HDL simulation. The HDL testbench completion logic is considering the HDL testbench simulation to be complete one clock cycle before the final output of the design is generated. Therefore, the testbench value in the last cycle is not being validated.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when generating code for a reusable Function-Call Subsystem fed by a virtual bus
Description: MATLAB may crash when generating code for a model when all four of the following conditions are present: A reusable Function-Call Subsystem has a virtual bus input The virtual bus contains elements of different data types The subsystem's parent is an atomic subsystem The virtual bus originates outside the atomic (parent) subsystem
Workaround: Change the virtual bus to a nonvirtual bus by manually inserting a Signal Conversion block (either outside the parent subsystem or inside the parent subsystem) or by configuring the bus to nonvirtual at its source.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier harnesses created for some models with tunable parameters and model references do not behave as expected
Description: Design Verifier harnesses created for models that satisfy all  of the following conditions do not show the expected simulation behavior:  The model references another model. The model has parameter configuration enabled. The referenced model has at least one configured parameter.
Workaround: If Design Verifier analysis has not started: Load only the top model to be analysed by Design Verifier. Do not load any of the referenced models.For the top model, select the Analysis > Design Verifier > Options > Results > Save test harness as model parameter.Start Design Verifier analysis.         The harness model created after the analysis will show the correct behavior.If Design Verifier analysis is complete: Use the sldvruntest function to simulate the test cases or counter examples in the generated sldvData file.

Summary: Simulating a generated test-case might result in a Simulink error.
Description: A Simulink error is thrown when attempting to simulate a generated test case under the following conditions: Model contains Lookup Table blocks whose entries are specified using parameters. The Lookup Table parameters have constraints specified on them. The generated test case results in Lookup Table entries that are not monotonically increasing.  The following error is thrown when simulating the test case:  Parameter values of InputValues in 'ModelName/Look-Up Table' must be monotonically increasing before and after conversion to its run-time data type.
Workaround: Disable constraints for Lookup Table parameters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initial value from Simulink.Signal object does not appear in generated code for output of Variant Subsystem or Concatenate block.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when all of the following conditions apply: A Simulink.Signal object with a non-zero initial value is attached to the output signal of a Variant Subsystem or a Concatenate block. The data type of the output signal of the Variant Subsystem or Concatenate block is not double . For the case of a Variant Subsystem, at least one the choices is a non-virtual (atomic or conditionally executed) subsystem. For the case of a Concatenate block, at least one of the input signals of the Concatenate block is driven by a non-virtual subsystem.  When the above conditions are met, the initialization code for the initial value of the Simulink.Signal object will not be generated for elements of the Concatenate block that are originating from such non-virtual subsystems.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block at the output port of the Variant Subsystem or Concatenate block and move the Simulink.Signal object from the output port of the Concatenate block to the output port of the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: Some content of axes clipped when saving to PS or EPS files
Description: If you save axes to PS or EPS files, the right side of the axes might be missing when you open the PS or EPS file in an external viewer, or when you insert/import the EPS file into Word as an image. The clipped content is due to a tight bounding box used when saving the axes.
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1349402 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues that exist in MATLAB R2015a and R2015aSP1.Visit Bug Report ID 1349473 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues in R2015a and R2015aSP1 compiled applications.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Indexing into a 1-by-1-by-N matrix with a nonscalar index produces a different answer in the generated code than in MATLAB
Description: The generated code may produce an answer that differs from MATLAB for a matrix subscript operation A(L)  if all of the following conditions are true: A is a multi-dimensional matrix of size 1-by-1-by-N, 1-by-1-by-1-by-N, and so on.  L is not a scalar.    Specifically, the values in A(L)  are the same as in MATLAB, but the orientation of the computed vector is different.  For example, suppose that A  has size?1-by-1-by-12 and L  has size 10-by-1. In? MATLAB, the size of A(L)  is 1-by-1-by-10, but in the generated code, the size of A(L)  is 10-by-1.  To see an example of the issue: 1.?Write the function test.   function out = testA = reshape(1:12, [1 1 12]);L = 1:10';out = size(A(L));end   2.?Generate a MEX function for test. ?codegen test   In MATLAB, test  produces [1 1 10], but , test_mex , produces [10 1]. This issue can also occur in a MATLAB Function block and a MATLAB function in Stateflow.
Workaround: Avoid using arrays that have more than one leading singleton dimension, such as 1-by-1-by-N.

Summary: Simulation targets are rebuilt unnecessarily
Description: Updating a model may result in all Simulation targets being rebuilt uneccessarily when all  of these conditions exist: On a machine with Simulink Coder installed, you have an updated model that contains model references or the model is simulated in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode. You access the model and its slprj  folder from a computer that does not have Simulink Coder installed.  ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Overwriting an open dictionary file can result in incorrect values being used by Simulink
Description: If you open a data dictionary file in Simulink and then copy a different data dictionary file into the same location, Simulink might not recognize that the file has changed and continue using the data from the original dictionary file.?
Workaround: Avoid copying dictionary files.? Instead, you can make changes to the existing dictionary file by using the data dictionary's command-line API.

Summary: Unused arguments of exported graphical functions might be incorrectly optimized away
Description: The code generator might incorrectly optimize away the unused function arguments of an exported graphical function. For exported graphical functions that Simulink can call, removing these unused arguments from the exported function declaration might lead to uncompilable code or compiler warnings. For example, in the generated code for the attached model (ExportedGraphicalFunctionUnusedArgs.slx ), the function declaration in MyExportedFcn.h  does not match the function callsite in ExportedGraphicalFunctionUnusedArgs.c because the unused function argument,  FcnMode, is incorrectly optimized out at the function callsite.
Workaround: Wrap the unused function argument with an empty function, Then, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, supply an empty function definition as part of the Custom code pane. For example, in the attached model, in Chart1, uncomment out this line:?//UNUSED(FcnMode);?Then, generate code. Notice that FcnMode is no longer optimized out of the generated code at the function callsite, and the Custom code pane contains an empty definition for UNUSED.

Summary: Probabalistic roadmap path planner uses incorrect connection distance
Description: The Probabilistic Roadmap (robotics.PRM ) path planner uses a different connection distance value than what is specified in the ConnectionDistance  for the construction of the roadmap, when the supplied binary occupancy grid (robotics.BinaryOccupancyGrid ) has resolution other than 1. For example, the problem manifests itself in the following code segment : resolution = 10;  desiredConnectionDistance = 2;  map = robotics.BinaryOccupancyGrid(5, 5, resolution);  prm = robotics.PRM(map);  prm.ConnectionDistance = desiredConnectionDistance;  show(prm)  In the figure, you will see that distances between some of the connected nodes are larger than the desired connection distance of 2. The PRM object uses the connection distance of 20 instead.
Workaround: The above behavior is caused because ConnectionDistance is calculated as the product of ConnectionDistance and the binary occupancy grid's resolution. In the example above, a map resolution of 10 and desired connection distance of 2 resulted in an actual connection distance of 20. As a workaround to this issue, you can specify the ConnectionDistance to be the quotient of the originally desired connection distance and the map resolution. For example, the above code could be modified as follows:resolution = 10;desiredConnectionDistance = 2;map = robotics.BinaryOccupancyGrid(5, 5, resolution);prm = robotics.PRM(map);prm.ConnectionDistance = desiredConnectionDistance/resolution;show(prm)?

Summary: Simulink can report a sample time error when the solver step size of a referenced model is set to 'auto'
Description: If a referenced model contains only Simulink Function blocks and the solver step size of the referenced model is set 'auto', then Simulink can produce a diagnostic message such as: The sample time period (0.05) of 'MyReferencedModel' is not an integer multiple of the fixed step size (1) specified for model. Simulink should treat 'auto' as a valid setting and pick the correct sample time.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box of the referenced model, set the Fixed-step size (fundamental sample time) equal to the Fixed-step size of the top model.

Summary: MATLAB? hangs on Mac platform when you change the working directory quickly
Description: The MATLAB? desktop on Mac platform hangs when you change the working directory quickly. The hang occurs when you change the working directory before the Current Folder browser finishes displaying icons for the files and folders of the previous working directory.
Workaround: Turn off the Current Folder browser's use of native icons:Enter this command in the command window: com.mathworks.services.Prefs.setBooleanPref('DocumentsNoNativeIcons', true);Restart MATLAB?.

Summary: Build error or incorrect executable for parallel model rebuild
Description: A top model build in which referenced models are built in parallel can fail or build incorrectly if all  of the following conditions are true: You build the top model successfully, including parallel building of referenced models. After the build, you change a configuration option or parameter for the referenced models. You initiate another build of the top model.  Under these conditions, the build can fail with an error or the generated executable can behave incorrectly. Note: This issue is caused by an incorrectly generated makefile and is not an issue with the generated code itself. Generated code compiled outside of the Simulink Coder build process is not impacted.
Workaround: To work around this bug, delete the code generation folder between successive parallel model builds.To fix the bug, install the attached patch**.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.**Notes for R2015a:It is recommended to follow the workaround instructions in bug report 1199590, as both these bugs are closely related.

Summary: Mixed data type error for model containing virtual buses and alias types
Description: An error can appear when you update a diagram for a model that uses both virtual buses and alias data types. The error looks similar to this: Data type mismatch. Output port 1 of 'model/From26' is a signal of mixed data type. However, it is driving a signal of mixed data type. The error appears if the model meets all of these conditions:1. The model contains virtual buses2. The virtual buses contain signals whose data types are defined by Simulink.NumericType  objects whose IsAlias  property is set to true , or by Simulink.AliasType  objects3. The signals define a feedback loop
Workaround: Modification of Simulink AliasType objectsOne workaround is to replace Simulink.AliasType objects with Simulink.NumericType objects, and set the IsAlias property of the new objects to false. Set the IsAlias property of existing Simulink.NumericType objects to false. If you used the data type objects to control data type names in the generated code, you can instead use the objects to configure data type replacements. To configure data type replacements for code generation, see Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Data Type Replacement.Binary patch for Windows and R2014bIf your platform is Windows and you are using R2014b, a second workaround is to install the attached binary patch. Summary: Indexing into static methods or constructors within a script errors
Description: If you index into a static method or constructor from within a script, MATLAB reports the following error: Static method or constructor invocations cannot be indexed.  The same code does not error if you enter it at the command prompt. For example, the following code results in an error if it is called from within a script, but not if it is called from the command prompt: hostname = java.net.InetAddress.getLocalHost().getHostName();
Workaround: Do not index into static methods from within scripts.  For example, inside of a script, instead of using the above code use:hostname = java.net.InetAddress.getLocalHost().getHostName();orlocalhost = java.net.InetAddress.getLocalHost();hostname = localhost.getHostName();

Summary: MATLAB crashes when applying Code Generation Advisor recommendations and regenerating code
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model if all of the following conditions are true: Code has already been successfully generated for the model? The Code Generation Advisor has already been run on the model Model configuration parameters were changed after the Code Generation Advisor run

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Incorrect code generation for partitioned parameter used by nested For Each subsystem
Description: A model generates incorrect code if all of these conditions are true: The model contains a For Each subsystem. This subsystem partitions some mask parameter. The For Each subsystem contains another For Each subsystem. This inner subsystem contains blocks that refer to the mask parameter that the outer subsystem partitions. The inner subsystem is reusable in the generated code. For example, on the Code Generation  tab of the subsystem parameters dialog box, you set Function packaging  to Reusable function . The outer subsystem is set to generate non-reusable function .
Workaround: Open the subsystem parameters dialog box for the outer For Each subsystem. On the Code Generation tab, set Function packaging to Inline.

Summary: DOM API open method fails with Window UNC network path for the template path
Description: The Document  class open  method fails when the template path is a Windows UNC network path. For example: doc = Document(report_path, type, '\\SERVER\Path\TemplateName');open(doc); % this fails
Workaround: Add the file extension .dotx or .htmtx to the template path. For example:switch(lower(type))    case 'docx'        ext = 'dotx';     case 'html'         ext = 'htmtx';     case 'html-file'         ext = 'html'; end template = ['\\SERVER\Path\TemplateName.' ext]; doc = Document(report_path, type, template); open(doc); % this worksAlternatively, you can install the attached patch.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the .zip file atached to this Bug Report that corresponds to the word length of your Windows system (win32 or win64).Store the downloaded file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file, open the .zip file and look at the contents. Note that you must store the backup copy in a directory that differs from the directory containing the original.Extract the files from the .zip file.Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initialize value of Merge block is different between simulation and code generation workflows
Description: The initialize value of a Merge block is different between simulation and code generation workflows when any one of these conditions does not  exist: The Merge block is driven by one of these: i) a block inside an unconnected triggered subsystem, ii) a function call subsystem, or  iii) a Stateflow chart with trigger input port. The block that drives the Merge block sets a non-zero Initial Condition (IC) value at the output port of the driving block. The Merge block sets its own non-zero IC value.  ?
Workaround: Do not use an unconnected triggered or function-call subsystem to drive the Merge block.

Summary: The tar and untar functions incorrectly process files with path names longer than 100 characters.
Description: The tar  function issues an error when it encounters files with path names exceeding 100 characters.   The untar  function treats files with path names exceeding 100 characters according to the tar file type: USTAR tar files - extracts the files into a truncated path and does not issue an error or warning GNU tar files - does not extract the files and issues a warning

Summary: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for Constant block input to Bus Assignment block
Description: Simulink Code Inspector returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if a Bus Assignment block uses a Constant block to replace a bus element.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect normal simulation results might occur for Subsystem block in algebraic loop
Description: Normal simulation results might be incorrect for a model that meets both of these conditions:You simulate the model in normal mode using the sim  command-line option (for example, sim(bdroot,'simulationmode','normal')).  The model contains a subsystem which is in an algebraic loop.
Workaround: Simulate the model using the Run button on the toolbar.

Summary: Assorted Model Slicer issues in dialog, generating slice models, and targeting slicing to a subsystem
Description: This bug report includes patches for the following Model Slicer bugs found in R2015a:  Sliced model generation can fail under any of the following conditions:Original model contains a referenced model and uses Configuration Reference Inactive elements of bus signals are grounded in the sliced model and error messages are thrown in Diagnostic Viewer due to name mismatch A root-level input port uses enumerated data type and is inactive The initial value of an inactive subsystem output of enumerated data type is used by a downstream block A disconnected line remains in the sliced model for input signals of an If block and a SwitchCase block A disconnected line remains in the sliced model when the second port of a Multiport Switch block is inactive and its upstream block is a subsystem Inactive lines between a Mux block and a root-level inport are retained in the sliced model   The sliced model produces false-positive warning messages about sorted execution order changes when the sliced model contains Data Store Memory blocks Some parts are unintentionally accessible in the Model Slice Manager dialog if the model fails to compile when Model Slicer is invoked Inactive input signals for a Merge block are highlighted when the simulation time window is applied with downstream signal propagation Issues when a slice subsystem is specified: Downstream highlighting throws an error about Struct contents reference from a non-struct array object  when the  slice subsystem contains another subsystem. Simulation time window cannot be applied if a slice subsystem is selected from a referenced model. Simulation time window cannot be applied if a slice subsystem contains a Data Store Read or Write block whose signal dimension is neither scalar nor 1xN.

Summary: Model Loop reporting on many model references can hang MATLAB
Description: When a report containing a Model Loop component reports on a model that contains many referenced models, report generation can hang MATLAB.

Summary: Deleting a For-Each Subsystem block during InitFcn callback of another subsystem crashes Simulink
Description: In the InitFcn  callback of a parent subsystem, if you try to delete a child For-Each Subsystem block, Simulink crashes when you update the model (Ctrl+D). For example, this InitFcn  callback of a parent subsystem causes Simulink to crash when  you update the model: if strcmp(get_param(gcs,'SimulationStatus'),'updating')  delete_block([gcs, '/ForEachSubsystem' ])  end    ?
Workaround: Contact MathWorks support if this issue impacts you. Please create a Service Request at: https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.htmlWhen you create a Service Request, please provide your license number, MATLAB version, and operating system. You can get this information by typing 'ver' at the command prompt. Technical Support requests require that you are enrolled in our Software Maintenance Service.

Summary: Simulink may produce an invalid diagnostic when simulating a Simulink Function block in rapid accelerator mode
Description: If a Simulink Function block contains an Argument Inport block that is connected directly to a Scope block or to a Stateflow chart, Simulink may report a diagnostic such as: Unable to build a standalone executable to simulate the model 'MyModel' in rapid accelerator mode. The signal leaving the Argument Inport block 'My/Simulink Function/u' must not be testpointed or have a non-auto storage class.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block between the Argument Input block and the Scope block or Stateflow chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for MATLAB System block in code generation mode when tunable properties are changed from within the System object
Description: When a MATLAB System block is simulating via code generation and the associated System object modifies a tunable property from within the System object code, the property is changed back to the value currently specified in the MATLAB System Block Dialog Parameters on the next time step.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code can occur when a root-level output signal is specified as reusable and is driven by a Constant block
Description: Incorrect code is generated when all of the conditions are true: A pair of root-level input and output signal objects are specified as Custom (Reusable)  storage class. The root output signal is driven by a Constant block with a constant (inf ) sample time. The root output signal is being read at some non-constant sample time.  ?
Workaround: Remove the Custom (Reusable) storage class specification for Simulink.signal object associated with the pair of root-level I/O signals.

Summary: Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) Simulation crash may occur for model using Simulink Function blocks and configured function prototypes
Description: If your model contains a Simulink Function block and a Function specification  of  Model specific C prototypes , a SIL simulation crash may occur. ?
Workaround: Replace the TLC file at $(matlabroot)/rtw/c/tlc/mw/codeinfofcnlib.tlc with the TLC file in the attached zip.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changes to properties of fixed-point inputs not applied??
Description: In the MATLAB?  Coder? app, if you do not commit a change to a property of a fixed-point input, code generation does not apply the change. The changes are committed only if you press Enter  or click outside of the text field.
Workaround: Using the MATLAB? Coder? app, after making changes to any properties of fixed-point inputs, in the text field, press Enter.

Summary: Dashboard blocks inside custom library instances lose settings and connection
Description: This bug affects custom library workflows involving Dashboard blocks. When a custom library that contains Dashboard blocks is instantiated in a model, any change to the Dashboard block settings and connection is lost when the model is saved and closed. The next time the model is opened, the Dashboard block shows the Double-click to connect  message, indicating that the Dashboard block is not connected. Additionally, when reopened, the model shows unsaved changes exist even though no changes were made.

Summary: When you apply changes from the Simulink Function block dialog box, MATLAB can crash
Description: When you click Apply  or OK  on the dialog box of a Simulink Function block that is inside a Stateflow chart, MATLAB can crash.
Workaround: Before clicking on Apply or OK, navigate out of the parent Stateflow chart in all open editors.

Description: When an AUTOSAR export-function model reuses local Goto and From block tags at different model levels, one of two possible errors occurs. For example, an error occurs if a model includes Goto and From blocks that use tag MYTAG  at root /Subsystem1  and includes Goto and From blocks that use the same tag at root/Subsystem1/Subsystem1 . The errors that can occur are: Maximum recursion limit. For example, Maximum recursion limit of 500 reached. Use set(0,'RecursionLimit',N) to change the limit. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling a Simulink function with variable-sized arrays from Stateflow produces incorrect results
Description: When calling a Simulink function that accepts variable sized arrays from Stateflow, the variable-sized array size is always marked as zero instead of the current size.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL code generation for Discrete FIR Filter block fails
Description: HDL code generation for Discrete FIR Filter block fails if DSP System Toolbox is not installed. HDL code generation returns the following error: MATLAB:UndefinedFunction: Undefined function 'getLegacyDigitalFilterParameter' for input arguments of type 'char'. HDL code generation for this filter should not require DSP System Toolbox.
Workaround: To solve this issue, unzip getLegacyDigitalFilterParameter.zip to a folder on the MATLAB path. This workaround applies to releases R2014b and R2015a.

Summary: Default delimiter in textscan does not include whitespace characters
Description: When not explicitly specified, the value of the 'Delimiter'  parameter of the textscan  function uses the value of the 'Whitespace'  parameter. However, when you set a custom value for 'Whitespace'  that includes a single space character (char(32) ), the whitespace character is not included as a delimiter character.
Workaround: To specify delimiter characters, use the 'Delimiter' name-value pair argument instead of the 'Whitespace' argument.For example, do this:textscan(fid,format,'Delimiter',{' ',','},'MultipleDelimsAsOne',true)instead of this:textscan(fid,format,'Whitespace',' ,')

Summary: Incremental parallel build might fail after model changes that affect code generation
Description: A parallel build of a model might fail if the following conditions exist: Generated files from a previous parallel build persist in the build folder. You made changes to a model that is referenced by the top model from which you are generating code, and those changes affect the code interface of the model. For example, you added or removed a root port of the child model, which changes its generated model interface.  Under these conditions, the build process might fail to correctly reconcile the model changes with the build folder contents. The build fails with compilation or linking errors. ?
Workaround: To work around this bug, you can remove the build folder before starting the parallel build, or apply the attached fix.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Summary: Crash when executing code generated for model involving both linear and nonlinear physical systems
Description: Invalid code is generated for linear physical systems that are combined with nonlinear physical systems in one Simulink(TM) model. The invalid code is generated for Rapid Accelerator and for all Simulink Real-Time(TM) targets. Executing the code results in a crash (Invalid memory access).
Workaround: To work around the issue, introduce nonlinearity into the linear part of the model. For example, add a PS Saturation block, from the Physical Signals/Nonlinear Operators library, to a physical signal connection line. To preserve the simulation results, specify the block saturation limits as -inf and inf.?

Summary: MATLAB can crash when you copy or paste a block that has a test harness whose name conflicts with an open Simulink model
Description: If you copy a block that has a test harness, and the test harness name conflicts with the name of an open Simulink model, MATLAB crashes. Also, if you paste a block with one or more test harnesses, and a new auto-generated test harness name conflicts with an open Simuink model, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output as nonvirtual bus in parent model may be ignored for an Outport of a Variant Model block
Description: If you have a Variant Model block with Data type  in the choice blocks set to  Bus and the parameter?Output as nonvirtual bus in parent model  is set inconsistently for same output across choices, then the parameter setting is ignored and the bus emerging in the?Variant model is nonvirtual.

Summary: Simulink Desktop Real-Time models do not run in real time on Windows 8 or Windows 8.1
Description: On some computers running Windows 8 or Windows 8.1, Simulink Desktop Real-Time models can fail to run in real time. In Normal Mode, models run very slowly and the software reports a timeout warning to the MATLAB Command Window. In External Mode, models do not run at all. This bug does not affect computers running Windows 7.
Workaround: To work around the issue, please enter the following commands once before starting the first real-time simulation:k = RT.Kernel;k.DebugConfig(1) = 2;reinitialize(k);You can add these commands to the startup file (startup.m or matlabrc.m) to run automatically on MATLAB startup.

Summary: fmincon stalls, and eventually hits the MaxFunEvals tolerance
Description: On some numerically difficult problems, the 'sqp'  algorithm in fmincon  can introduce NaN s into the vector of parameters, x . In this case, the algorithm gets stuck in a line-search subproblem, leading to a stall that ends when the number of function evaluations exceeds the MaxFunEvals  tolerance. To see whether this issue affects your solution, write an output function that checks or displays the x  value, such as: stop = outfun(x, optimValues, state)stop = false;if max(isnan(x(:)) > 0) disp(['There is a NaN at iteration',num2str(optimValues.iteration)])end Include the output function by setting the OutputFcn  option to @outfun .
Workaround: The issue occurs when the problem is not numerically well-conditioned. This can be the result of poor model formulation, or in some cases, poor scaling. To attempt to work around this issue:Turn on scaling in the algorithm by setting the ScaleProblem option to 'obj-and-constr'.Use the 'interior-point' algorithm instead of 'sqp'.Try suggestions in the "When the solver fails" section of the documentation.

Summary: Strings get unexpectedly larger when the output size exceeds the screen size
Description: Printing/saving a MATLAB figure with an output size greater than the screen size can cause strings to scale unexpectedly. This results in proportionally larger strings in the generated output. In the files generated below you will notice that the y-label and the axes-ticks appear larger in the "toobig.png" file than they do in either of the others. % create a figure and plot some datafigure;plot(rand(4));ylabel('Some interesting data');   % determine how big the screen is, in inches scrSize = get(0, 'ScreenSize') / get(0, 'ScreenPixelsPerInch');  % generate output that matches the screen size outputSize = [0 0 scrSize(3:4)];set(gcf,'PaperUnits', 'inches', 'PaperPosition', outputSize);print -dpng screensize.png;  % generate output that is smaller than the screen size smallerSize = outputSize - [0 0 2 2];set(gcf,'PaperUnits', 'inches', 'PaperPosition', outputSize);print -dpng smaller.png;  % generate output that is sized larger than the screen size bigSize = outputSize + [0 0 4 4];set(gcf,'PaperUnits', 'inches', 'PaperPosition', bigSize);print -dpng toobig.png;
Workaround: Users of  MATLAB version R2015a or R2015aSP1 can visit Bug Report ID 1349402 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues that exist in MATLAB R2015a and R2015aSP1.Users of applications compiled with MATLAB version R2015a or R2015aSP1 can visit Bug Report ID 1349473 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues in R2015a and R2015aSP1 compiled applications.Otherwise, reduce the output size so that it is screen size or smaller.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using importdata or textscan with CollectOutput and the %q format
Description: The textscan  function can crash MATLAB when CollectOutput = true , the format specifier contains two or more adjacent %q  specifiers, and the data is incomplete. This problem occurs for strings and files that end abruptly (i.e. end without a trailing new line character). The same behavior might result when reading similar files with importdata .
Workaround:
There are two workarounds:1. If you know the line ending for your file, add a new line character to the end of the file or string. Then, read the file or string using textscan or importdata. There is no need to modify the file or string again for subsequent read operations.Note: If the new line is something other than '
', use the correct new line for your file.EoL = '
';For files :fid = fopen(filename,'a');fprintf(fid,EoL);flcose(fid);For strings:str = sprintf(['%s' EoL],str);2. If you are unsure of the line ending in your file, call textscan with the 'EndOfLine','
' name-value pair argument to read the file. For example:fid = fopen(filename,'rt');data = textscan(fid,'%q%q','EndOfLine','
','CollectOutput',true);

Summary: Noncompilable code can be generated when models contain either an S-Function, Model block, or some built-in blocks
Description: Embedded Coder can generate noncompilable code for a model if all  of the following conditions are true: The model contains an S-Function, Model block, or some built-in blocks. The S-Function or Model block has an input from a Constant block. The Constant block has a mathematical expression written in its value field.  The built-in blocks include the Integrator Limited, Transport Delay, Variable Time Delay, Variable Transport Delay, Discrete State-Space, Discrete Zero-Pole, 1-D Lookup Table, 2-D Lookup Table, and n-D Lookup Table blocks.  Under these conditions, the generated code contains a dereference to a variable that is neither a pointer nor an array and the code cannot be compiled. This is likely to appear as a compiler error similar to:C2100: illegal indirection
Workaround: Use one of these approaches to work around this bug:Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Constant block and the problematic downstream block. In the Signal Conversion block, select the option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.Create a Test Point on the signal connecting the two blocks: right-click the signal, select Signal Properties,and then check Test Point.?

Summary: Memory leaks occur when opening new windows in MATLAB? on Mac
Description: The MATLAB? Desktop experiences memory leaks when running on a Mac operating system with an out of date version of Java. The problem occurs when opening new windows within MATLAB?. This includes figure windows, editor windows and preference windows. This is an issue with the underlying JDK (Java Development Kit). See the JDK bug report  and list of fixed bugs .
Workaround: Upgrade the JDK to Java 7 update 75 and point MATLAB? to that JDK. Download Java 7 update 75.Follow these instructions to point MATLAB? to the updated JDK.

Description: When you review analysis results for code generated with Embedded Coder, you can select certain links to highlight the related blocks in your model. If you have a coding rule violation and a back-to-model link on the same line of code, when you select the link, this error message can appear:  Error: No source code available for this item. Summary: Assertion when using variable-size arrays
Description: MATLAB?  can crash when generating C/C++ code from  MATLAB code that has the pattern  y = [y; u];  where  y  and u  are Nx1 matrices and u  is a variable-size array with all fixed-size dimensions. To see an example of this problem, generate code for the attached file. ?
Workaround: For a MATLAB statement having the formy = [y; u]; change y and u to 1xN or make u a fixed-size variable.

Summary: In Simcape language, literal values in 'deg' are handled incorrectly in the equation section
Description: In the equation section of a component file, if you specify a literal value in 'deg' , it is handled incorrectly. For example, { 180 'deg' } is actually treated as { 180 'rad' }. Angular velocity and other compound units involving angles are handled correctly.
Workaround: When using literal angular values, use radians.

Summary: Simulink.NumericType parameters are not listed in the Parameter Table in Simulink Design Verifier options
Description: In the Configuration Parameters > Design Verifier > Parameters  pane, the Find in Model  operation of the Parameter Table  does not list model parameters that are of type Simulink.NumericType . This prevents the application of constraints to these parameters for achieving full coverage.
Workaround: Constraints for Simulink.NumericType parameters can be specified using a Parameter Configuration File.

Summary: Execution timeline graphic from profile_xpc shows wrong core number for preempted task
Description: The profile_xpc  function generates a graphic that shows the sequence of task activation and preemption over the execution timeline. The function generates the graphic using data recorded during code execution on the target hardware. For multicore hardware, the data includes the number of the core on which each task runs on. In some cases, the graphic shows the wrong core number for a task that has resumed following preemption.

Summary: Code metrics are missing in the reports
Description: By default, Polyspace Ada results list code metrics along with the verification results. However, when you generate a report from the results, code metrics do not appear.
Workaround: In the Polyspace interface, open your project configuration.In the Configuration window, open the Advanced Settings pane.In the Other text field, enter -code-metrics.Relaunch the verification.

Summary: Copy and paste of both a subchart and supertransition causes a crash
Description: MATLAB can crash when you copy and paste a selection that includes both a Stateflow subchart and a supertransition.
Workaround: First copy and paste just the subchart(s), then the copy and paste the supertransition(s).

Summary: Setting the units of UI objects inside GUIDE to normalized can corrupt the UI file
Description: In GUIDE, changing the units of UI objects to normalized units can cause the UI file to become corrupt and lose data. The following must be true for this corruption to occur: The UI has container objects (such as a uipanel or uibuttongroup object) in a hierarchical sequence. For example, a uipanel is contained within another uipanel. You changed the units of a UI object to normalized units using one of the following techniques: Setting the Resize Behavior  of the UI to Proportional  in the GUI Options dialog. Setting the Units  property of a UI object to 'normalized' .   Using normalized units can have the following effects on the UI when displayed through GUIDE: The UI objects disappear from the UI. The position and layout information of UI objects change from their original configuration.  ?
Workaround: Recovering corrupted UI files:Although the UI objects appear to be missing, they still exist in GUIDE. You can recover some information in your UI file by first making the UI objects reappear, and then changing their units to something other than 'normalized'.To make the UI objects reappear, move the parent container slightly. For example, if the missing object is inside a uipanel, then select the uipanel and move it slightly using one of arrow keys on your keyboard.When the missing object becomes visible again, change its Units property to any value other than 'normalized' (for example, 'characters').Avoid setting the Units property back to 'normalized'. Otherwise the problem will reoccur when you save, close and reopen the file in GUIDE.You might need to adjust the layout of your UI after you restore the UI objects.Enable UI resizing after recovering corrupted UI files:To make your UI resize proportionally, open your program file, and add the following code to the function that has _OpeningFcn in the name. hfig = hObject;% Set the figure visible in order to make sure the rendering finisheshfig.Visible = 'on';drawnow;% Enable resize of the figurehfig.Resize = 'on';% Change Units of each object to normalizedhAllObjects = findobj(hfig);for i = 1:numel(hAllObjects)    hAllObjects(i).Units = 'normalized';endThe attached GUIDE files (exampleWorkaround.m and exampleWorkaround.fig) show how to implement the code above. In this case, the code is added to the function, exampleWorkaround_OpeningFcn in exampleWorkaround.m.For more assistance, contact Technical Support by visiting the following page: https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html

Summary: Results of some datetime operations involving leap seconds are off by 1 second
Description: Some calculations on datetime  variables set to the time zone, 'UTCLeapSeconds' , can return results that differ by 1 second from the correct results. For example, this code should return 2012-06-30T23:59:60.000Z : >> t = datetime(2012,6,30,23,59,0,'TimeZone','UTCLeapSeconds');>> t.Second = 60t =   2012-07-01T00:00:00.000Z This code should return 2012-07-01T00:00:00.000Z : >> t = datetime(2012,6,30,23,60,0,'TimeZone','UTCLeapSeconds')t =   2012-06-30T23:59:60.000Z This code should return 2013-06-30T23:59:59.000Z : >> t = datetime(2012,6,30,23,59,60,'TimeZone','UTCLeapSeconds');>> t = t + calyears(1)t =   2013-07-01T00:00:00.000Z
Workaround: Install the patch provided for Bug Report 1196109. That patch includes fixes for this bug.

Summary: Incorrect red check in unreachable code
Description: Consider the following pseudo code where all the if  branches are unreachable. However, Polyspace displays a Non-Terminating Call (NTC) red in the third branch. /* at init */a in range [true, false];b and c are false;...while() {      ...      if (a) {         if (b == true) { // should be unreachable code             c = true;         }  } else {         if (c == true) {// should be unreachable code             b = true;  }      }      if (b == true and c == true) // should be unreachable code           func(); // Red non terminating call      ...} Because the code is unreachable, the non-terminating call to func()  does not occur. Therefore, Polyspace incorrectly detects a red error on the call to func() . ?

Summary: Model reference hierarchy containing a non-inlined TLC S-Function that can be inlined inside a reusable subsystem might error out during accelerator mode simulation
Description: A model that is inside a model reference hierarchy may error out in accelerator mode if the following conditions exist: The model contains a subsystem with Function packaging  set to Reusable function . If the model errors out, you will get the following error message: Error: File: matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/globalsysvar.tlc Line: 121 Column: 9 Undefined identifier tsysRTM

Summary: dlmread might error when reading a file with mixed line endings
Description:

Summary: MIDI interface loses contact with attached MIDI devices, on Mac only
Description: The MIDI interface may lose contact with correctly attached MIDI devices after initially working correctly. To regain contact, restart MATLAB. This problem occurs only on Macs.  For example, multiple calls to midiid  may reproduce this problem, as shown below. The number of calls to midiid  varies.     >> midiid    Move the control you wish to identify; type ^C to abort.    Waiting for control message... done    ans =            1081    >> midiid    Move the control you wish to identify; type ^C to abort.    Waiting for control message... done    ans =            1081    >> midiid    Warning: could not find any attached MIDI input devices.     > In midiid at 32     ans =         []    >>
Workaround: Download the attached MATLAB file midifix.m. Run midifix each time you start MATLAB, before using any MIDI features. For instructions, see the midifix command-line help.If you have already encountered the problem in your MATLAB session, running midifix in that same session does will not help. You must restart MATLAB and then run midifix.
