Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when an unassigned structure array is passed to the?size function
Description: Passing?an?unassigned?structure array to the size  function in your MATLAB code causes?the generated code?to have?undefined behavior. This can cause the generated code for the size  function to return a random?value and, as a result,?behave differently than the?MATLAB code. MATLAB would error. For example, the MATLAB function size2(u) defined?below?has undefined behavior in generated code if u >= 1 . function y = size2(u)if u < 1    t = struct('a',1,'b',2);    x = repmat(t,1,3);endy = size(x,1)  % Result of size(x,1) is undefined if u >= 1. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: Before passing a structure array to size in your MATLAB code, define it along all possible execution paths.

Summary: If blocks with multiple Elseif conditions cause errors
Description: If you run Simulink Design Verifier on a model that has If blocks with multiple Elseif expressions, you might encounter the following error: Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: < model > is incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: Performance degradation in impoly methods
Description: Executing certain methods (such as setPosition  and setVerticesDraggable ) on an impoly  object can be slow.

Summary: Exporting chart-level functions from a Stateflow chart within a export-function model may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: You can export chart-level functions from a Stateflow chart within a export-function model. Open a Stateflow chart. From the Chart  menu, select Properties . In the chart propertied dialog box, select the Export Chart Level Function (Make Global)  check box. Clear the Allow function to be called from Simulink  check box. At the MATLAB command prompt, enter clear mex . Delete  *_sfun.mex*  files from your working directory.  However, there are limitations to calling the exported functions. You can call the exported functions only from another Stateflow chart. The calling Stateflow charts must be located in the same model, not elsewhere in the model hierarchy.  Failing to clear the Allow function to be called from Simulink  check box will cause MATLAB to crash when you run a simulation.
Workaround: Clear the Allow function to be called from Simulink check box, and call the exported chart-level function from another Stateflow chart within the same model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for System Outputs and System Update blocks used in muxed function-call subsystems
Description: The code generator produces incorrect code for custom code blocks System Outputs and System Update when all of these conditions exist for a model: Multiple asynchronous function calls (for example, S-Function blocks) feed into a Mux block, which in turn connects to an asynchronous Function-Call Subsystem. The asynchronous Function-Call Subsystem contains multiple non-virtual subsystems (for example, Atomic, Enabled, Triggered, or Function-Call Subsystems). The non-virtual subsystems contain a System Outputs or System Update block.

Summary: Incorrect display of mask contents due to TeX formatting commands in block masks
Description: If you create a custom mask on a Simulink block and use one of the following TeX formatting commands in its content, the mask may be drawn incorrectly onscreen. \it (italic)  -- has no effect. \sl (slanted)  -- has no effect.  ?

Summary: USRP support package installation can error out when installing the dd v0.5 for Windows third-party software
Description: Installing the USRP support package on Windows can trigger an error such as this: Error installing third party software dd v0.5 for Windows: Error executing command "unzip('C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download\dd-0.5.zip', fullfile(sdruroot,'uhdapps','utils'))" Invalid zip file "C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download\dd-0.5.zip".
Workaround: Download the USRP support package to a folder (for example, C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download).In the folder, delete the dd-0.5.zip file. It is not a valid zip file.Use a web browser to download dd-0.5.zip manually from http://www.chrysocome.net/downloads/dd-0.5.zip to C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download.Install the USRP support package from the folder C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated for Sum or Add block implementing 1-bit subtraction
Description: HDL Coder may generate incorrect HDL code for a Sum or Add block implementing 1-bit subtraction.
Workaround: Modify the block to use a different implementation. One possibility is to perform a two-bit subtraction. A second option is to implement the operation directly in logic as: OUTPUT = IN1 XOR (NOT IN2); For R2014b only, you can also install the attached patch that addresses this bug as well as the following issues:Bug report 1123120Bug report 1127061Bug report 1132406Bug report 1134355Bug report 1135659Bug report 1140073Bug report 1156535Bug report 1162671Bug report 1167090Bug report 1167094Bug report 1168881Bug report 1196498Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                     Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell :unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems*:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  *Notes for Windows Systems:      You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.    If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you  may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to  WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting  Run as administrator.    From  within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.    Extract  the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: In matlab.wsdl.createWSDLClient, WSDLs that import other WSDLs can fail on Windows.
Description: When running the function you may get an error like:    Error using matlab.wsdl.createWSDLClient (line 238)    Error processing WSDL:    Failed to generate local wsdl for clientjar  if the WSDL file contains an import element like this:    <wsdl:import namespace="http://abc.org/MyService/ExternalWSDL" location="http://localhost/WebApplication1/WebService1.asmx?wsdl=wsdl1"/>  where the location attribute value contains a ?  character or perhaps some other character that is illegal in a file name.
Workaround: Use webread or a browser to download the WSDL file locally, as well as the all the WSDL files named in the location attributes of any wsdl:import elements in that file.  If the downloaded files contain additional wsdl:import references, download those files as well, and so on.  Once all the files are downloaded, edit the files to change the location attributes of all the import elements to point to the local file paths.If any of the imported WSDL files contain a wsdl:types element, you may also need to implement the workaround to 1183791.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code replacement library can cause code generator to replace functions that require model root input or output variables to be aligned at specific memory address boundaries
Description: The code generator can produce an executable that returns unexpected numerical results, crashes, or both when all the following conditions are met:    The compiler for the target processor produces?compiled code that gives numeric?results that differ from simulation results?or crashes when using an instruction that requires specific data alignments?and the data is not aligned properly. The code generator uses a code replacement library that replaces an operation or function with an implementation function that takes an argument with a data alignment requirement, and the argument uses the address of a root level I/O variable.   The model root input and output variables are not aligned at the required memory address boundaries.   The model is configured for the code generator to do one of the following:              Produce code for the AUTOSAR system target file.           Produce code for an Embedded Coder system target file with the code interface packaging configured as:                          Nonreusable function interface with function prototype control enabled  (Code Generation > Interface > Code interface packaging  is set to Nonreusable function  and you use Code Generation > Interface > Configure Model Functions  to open the Model Interface dialog box and configure a custom function interface).                Reusable function interface with I/O passed in as individual arguments (Code Generation > Interface > Code interface packaging  is set to Reusable function  and Code Generation > Interface > Pass root-level I/O as  is set to Individual arguments ).                C++ class interface, using the I/O argument style interface (Code Generation > Interface > Code interface packaging  is set to C++ class,you use    Code Generation > Interface > Configure C++ Class Interface  to open the Configure C++ Class Interface dialog box, and you?set Function specification ?to I/O arguments step method ).                               Check whether you need to align input or output variables at specific memory address boundaries.

Summary: Deleting a Subsystem block in an open Simulink diagram can crash MATLAB
Description: When you delete a Subsystem block that has Simscape ports from a Simulink diagram (either in a model or library) that is open in a Simulink Editor window, there is a small chance that MATLAB?will crash.
Workaround: To delete that Subsystem block, do not open the system. Instead, load the system and use the command-line API to delete the block. For example:?load_system('sf_car')delete_block('sf_car/transmission')?

Summary: matlab.wsdl.createWSDLClient fails when a new type is decfined in the schema that is another name for a basic XML type.  Error using matlab.wsdl.createWSDLClient>getJavaClassForType (line 2556)  Assertion failed.
Description: matlab.wsdl.createWSDLClient  fails when the schema in the WSDL renames an XML basic type. An example is a schema that contains a line like:   <element name="newByte" nillable="true" type="xs:byte" /> which defines xs:newByte  as a nillable  form of the XML schema basic type byte .
Workaround: The only workaround is to download the WSDL file locally and edit it to replace references to the redefined type with references to the basic type.  In the above example, all references to newByte (possibly qualified as tns:newByte) should be replaced by xs:byte.

Summary: Errors or warnings in matlab.wsdl.createWSDLClient for WSDL files that import other WSDL files
Description: matlab.wsdl.createWSDLClient  may encounter an error condition when: the WSDL file imports another WSDL file the imported file defines or imports schemas  Files with WSDL file imports contain an import  element within the definitions  element, and files that contain schemas will have schema  elements within a types  element. The error or warning message may be similar to one of the following:     Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.     The WSDL file imports other WSDL files.  If these imported files    define any schema types, use of those types may generate errors.
Workaround: First, if the imported files are not local, copy them locally and alter the import elements of all the files to point to the local files.  To fix the problem you need to move all the schema definitions from the imported files into the top level file. These schema definitions appear as schema elements inside types elements.For example the top level file may have:    <definitions>         <import location="http://foo/bar?wsdl" />         <types>            ...top level type definitions...         </types>     </definitions> Download the file mentioned in the location attribute above (http://foo/bar?wsdl) to a local file and save it as imported_file. This file may contain:     <wsdl:types>         ...low level type definitions...         <xsd:schema>             ...schema definitions...         </xsd:schema>     </wsdl:types> The prefixes wsdl and xsd may vary or not appear at all. (import elements within the schema definitions do not need to be downloaded.) If there are any schema elements in the imported file, move them, including the opening and closing tags, from the imported file to the end of the types element of the top level WSDL file, deleting them from the imported files. (Leave alone existing schema elements in the top level file.) If the top level file already has a types section within it, just add the schema element(s) to the existing types content. Otherwise create a types section within the definitions element of the top level file to contain the schema elements.In the above example, the top level file would now contain:     <definitions>         <import location="imported_file" />         <types>             ...top level type definitions...             <xsd:schema>                ...schema definitions...             </xsd:schema>         </types>     </definitions> and imported_file will contain:     <wsdl:types>         ...low level type definitions...     </wsdl:types> When you are done you should have exactly one types element in the top level file inside the definitions element, containing all the schema defined in the imported WSDL files, and none of the imported WSDL files should contain any schema elements.

Summary: Offline-linked Simulink custom library entry can cause MATLAB to crash when the path to the library subsystem is resolved
Description: If a Simulink model contains a subsystem that is located in a custom library, and that subsystem has been renamed in the library but not in the model, resolving the path to the library via these steps can cause MATLAB to crash:  Double-click the "Unresolved Link" subsystem block  Correct the name of the source block in the Function Block Parameters: Subsystem dialog box to match the library block's new name.  Click OK  or Apply .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Time information inside nested function-call subsystems in Export-Function models is incorrect in generated code
Description: Incorrect code is generated when all the following conditions are true: The model is set up to export functions The model has a periodic function-call subsystem This periodic function-call subsystem contains no blocks which require absolute or elapsed time This periodic function-call subsystem contains a triggered function-call, if-action, or switch-case subsystem which contains blocks that require absolute or elapsed time  The clock tick in the generated code is not updated properly in this case. As a result, blocks in the nested subsystem do not have the correct time information. Attached are example models that expose this problem.
Workaround: Place a block which needs absolute time or elapsed time, such as the Digital Clock block, in the top-level periodic function-call subsystem.

Summary: Tab completion of field or property names can cause memory leaks
Description: Tab completion can cause a memory leak when attempting to complete the field of a structure or the property of an object containing classes. For example, entering the text myStruct.myFiel  in the Command Window and pressing the Tab  key can cause a memory leak if myStruct  contains classes in several of the fields.
Workaround: Do not use tab completion to complete structures containing classes in their fields or to complete objects containing classes in their properties. To resolve a memory leak, enter the error command in the Command Window. MATLAB reclaims leaked memory if an error is thrown.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Buffer passed into LibMdlStartCustomCode does not appear in the generated code
Description: When the LibMdlStartCustomCode  TLC function is called, the buffer created by that function is ignored.
Workaround: Before generating the code, run the attached .p file .This .p  file disable a feature present in R2014b that changes how initialization code is processed.

Summary: ROI tools in Image Processing Toolbox (impoint, imline, impoly, imrect, imellipse, imfreehand) do not respond appropriately to CLA function in R2015a.
Description: The ROI tools in the Image Processing Toolbox do not respond appropriately to the CLA function in R2015a. For example, after executing the following code: impoint(gca,0.5,0.5);cla(gca); The point remains in the axes, even though it should have been cleared by CLA. A second manifestation of this bug occurs in workflows in which IMSHOW is used with an existing axes. In all releases prior to R2015ad, the following code causes the existing ROI to be destroyed. In R2015a, the ROI remains drawn in the axes after the final call to IMSHOW. imshow('rice.png'); r = imrect(gca,[60 60 100 100]); imshow('cameraman.tif'); get(r) ?

Summary: Code generation?can fail to detect?the use of an unassigned?variable
Description: In MATLAB, using a variable that was not assigned before is an error.?This is also?true for code generation?from MATLAB. In certain cases, the?code?generator fails to detect?the use of a variable that is not assigned. In such cases, the code generator does not produce?a compilation error and proceeds with?code generation.? One specific?situation that is affected by this bug occurs when all of the following conditions?are true:? A?variable is assigned in one branch of a?if  or?switch  statement but not assigned in the other branch.  The variable is used only?inside size()  or numel() .  The size of the variable is fixed.   In this case, the?code generator takes the value of the variable?from the assignment in one branch, and uses it in?the other branch as well. For?example, consider this MATLAB function:? function out = foo(n)if n > 0    x = ones(3,4);elseendout = size(x,1);end? The intended behavior for code generation is to error ("Variable x is not assigned on some execution paths "), but instead the following C code is produced: out = 3;?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks?in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: To?determine if your Simulink model is affected by this issue, open the model in R2015b release or newer and perform update diagram (ctrl+D). If you do not receive a compilation error about variable used but not assigned, then your model is not affected.To?determine if your MATLAB source code (used with MATLAB Coder) is affected, use MATLAB Coder from R2015b release or newer. If code generation does not issue an error about variable used but not assigned, then your source code is not affected.?

Summary: Two Classification Learner icons in the apps gallery
Description: If you installed the tech preview for Classification Learner in R2014b, then in R2015a you see two Classification Learner icons in the apps gallery, unless you uninstalled the tech preview.
Workaround: The extra icon is a cosmetic problem only. You can use either of the two icons to open Classification Learner.

Summary: MATLAB crashes due to an illegal instruction when executing intlinprog or linprog
Description: On Windows, intlinprog  (R2014a and later) and the linprog  'dual-simplex' algorithm (R2014b and later) require the processor to support the SSE3 instruction set.  When these solvers are called on a computer that supports only earlier versions of the SSE instruction set such as SSE2, MATLAB can crash. Note that this requirement for SSE3 instruction set is more restrictive than the system requirements for MATLAB described in the following page: https://www.mathworks.com/support/sysreq/current_release/  For more information on SSE instructions and to determine which type your processor supports refer to the following article: https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/answers/93455-what-is-the-sse2-instruction-set-how-can-i-check-to-see-if-my-processor-supports-it

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer between simulation and generated code for Interpolation using Prelookup block with?Saturate on integer overflow?check box selected and?Intermediate results Data Type?set to fixed-point data type
Description: Incorrect?code might be generated from the Interpolation using Prelookup block?when these?conditions?are all true: The Saturate on integer overflow  check box is selected  The?Interpolation method ?is set to?Linear  Table Data Type ,?Intermediate results Data Type ,?and?Output Data Type ?are fixed-point data types Table Data Type ?and?Output Data Type ?have the same slope adjustment factor and bias Table Data Type ?does not overflow if cast to?Intermediate results Data type ?or the?Output Data Type   Intermediate results Data type ?may overflow if cast to?Output Data Type  The Integer Rounding mode  is set to either:Ceiling , Convergent , Round , or Nearest  when?the Table data  Value is close to the maximum value of the data type Floor , Simplest , or?Zero  when the Table data  Value is close to the minimum value of the data type    If these conditions are all present,?a wrapping effect may occur instead of a saturation effect in the output of the Interpolation using Prelookup block.
Workaround: Set the??Output data type?parameter of the Interpolation Using Prelookup block to?the same data type as the?Intermediate results data typeAdd and connect a?Data Type Conversion block to the output of the Interpolation Using Prelookup blockSelect the?Saturate on integer overflow?check box of the Data Type Conversion blockSet the?Output data type?parameter of the?Data Type Conversion block to the desired data type

Summary: MATLAB throws 'java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError' or 'Could not determine a temporary executable directory' error upon start
Description: MATLAB can throw the following errors upon start, after which plotting might not work: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: C:\Users\userName\AppData\Local\Temp\jogamp_0000\file_cache\jln7241190548013054463\jln8954501619229889979\gluegen-rt.dll: Access is deniedat java.lang.ClassLoader$NativeLibrary.load(Native Method)at java.lang.ClassLoader.loadLibrary1(Unknown Source)at java.lang.ClassLoader.loadLibrary0(Unknown Source)at java.lang.ClassLoader.loadLibrary(Unknown Source)at java.lang.Runtime.load0(Unknown Source)at java.lang.System.load(Unknown Source)at com.jogamp.common.jvm.JNILibLoaderBase.loadLibraryInternal(JNILibLoaderBase.java:530)... or Warning: Caught Exception while retrieving executable temp base directory: java.lang.RuntimeException: Could not determine a temporary executable directoryat com.jogamp.common.util.IOUtil.getTempDir(IOUtil.java:1391)at com.jogamp.common.util.cache.TempFileCache.(TempFileCache.java:80)at com.jogamp.common.util.cache.TempJarCache.initSingleton(TempJarCache.java:89)at com.jogamp.common.os.Platform$1.run(Platform.java:201)at java.security.AccessController.doPrivileged(Native Method)... These errors occur because either MATLAB is unable to locate a temporary directory from which it can execute, or it found the directory but is still unable to execute from within it.  The following are some of the reported causes that led to these errors: Lack of appropriate write/execute permissions Non-standard characters in the MATLAB installation directory Problems with the network directory where MATLAB is installed
Workaround: To correctly install the patch, follow these three steps:Download and install the patch files according to the instructions below.Create a java.opts file in your MATLAB path and add this command to the file: "-Djogamp.gluegen.UseTempJarCache=false". For more information on java.opts files, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_env/java-opts-file.html. If you have defined a java.opts file already, simply append the command to the existing file.   If you are using a Mac, simply append the command to the default java.opts file in the <MATLAB>/bin/maci64 directory.Navigate to the <MATLAB>/java/jarext directory. Append the word "BackUp" to the beginning of the gluegen-rt-natives-*.jar and jogl-all-natives-*.jar file names so that the file names are BackUpgluegen-rt-natives-*.jar and BackUpjogl-all-natives-*.jar.Installation of patch files:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Axes in docked figures resize after canceling print using Print dialog box
Description: Axes in a docked figure resize after canceling the print operation using the Print  dialog box. For example, create a plot and select File > Print...  from the figure menu. Then, select Cancel  in the Print  dialog box that appears. The axes in the figure might resize.
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1349402 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues that exist in MATLAB R2015a and R2015aSP1.Visit Bug Report ID 1349473 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues in R2015a and R2015aSP1 compiled applications.

Summary: Floating display does not work with output signal from root Inport block
Description: If you select the Floating display option for a Display block that has an input signal from a root Inport block, Simulink generates an error. The error message suggests three workarounds. However, those workarounds do not work for a Display block with input from a root Inport block.
Workaround: In releases after R2014b, use one of these approaches:- Use Data Display in Simulation instead of the floating display.Delete the floating display to avoid the error message when clicking on the root Inport block line.In the Simulink Editor, select Display->Data Display in Simulation->Toggle Value Labels When Clicked.Click the root Inport block signal line.- Keep the floating display for viewing other signals and use Display in Simulation to view signals from a root Inport blockIn the Simulink Editor, select Display->Data Display in Simulation->Toggle Value Labels When Hovering.Hover on the root Inport block signal line.Note: For model referencing, enable the Data Display in Simulation option in the referenced model.

Summary: GetOverloadCounter block does not return correct value for multitasking models.
Description: The GetOverloadCounter block does not return the correct value for multitasking models.
Workaround: If you have a multitasking model, do not develop an algorithm based on the value of GetOverloadCounter. The majority of our customers only use the SetOverloadCounter block to clear the overload counter in order to ignore the overload condition.If you want to investigate the overloads, please use the profiling tool, which shows more details.

Summary: Function pow may return incorrect analysis results when second argument is a nonpositive integer
Description: When the second input argument of pow  function is a nonpositive integer, Simulink Design Verifier may return incorrect analysis result.

Summary: The output of datestr contains periods at the end of short names for months for certain non-English locales, depending on the release of MATLAB

Summary: Simulink can crash when you change the name of a block
Description: Simulink can crash when you change the name of a block using the editor.
Workaround: One possible workaround is to change the name of the block using set_param. Click on the block in the editor.Go to the Command Window.Enter the following command, substituting the desired block name for newName:      set_param(gcb,'Name','newName'), .

Description: MATLAB might crash resulting in a crash log that contains the following information: Stack Trace (from fault):...[  8] 0x00000001114e95e5 /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+03991013 _Z33inClearArraysInCurrentAndGlobalWSPFP15inWorkSpace_tagiE+00000021[  9] 0x000000011120220a /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+00946698 _ZN12InterpBridge12FullClearFcnEiPP11mxArray_tagiS2_+00003050[ 10] 0x0000000111201d0d /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+00945421 _ZN12InterpBridge12FullClearFcnEiPP11mxArray_tagiS2_+00001773[ 11] 0x00000001108ddcc6 /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_dispatcher.dylib+00117958 _ZN11Mfh_builtin11dispatch_mfEiPP11mxArray_tagiS2_+00000086[ 12] 0x00000001108c612d /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_dispatcher.dylib+00020781 _ZN13Mfh_MATLAB_fn11dispatch_fhEiPP11mxArray_tagiS2_+00000333[ 13] 0x000000011128ed62 /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+01523042 _Z19inDispatchFromStackiPKcii+00001650[ 14] 0x0000000111230dd8 /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+01138136 inPcodeWalker+00022552[ 15] 0x000000011122d917 /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+01124631 inPcodeWalker+00009047[ 16] 0x0000000111228fb7 /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+01105847 inInterPcode+00000935[ 17] 0x000000011122a1c6 /Applications/MATLAB_R2015a.app/bin/maci64/libmwm_interpreter.dylib+01110470 _Z26inExecuteMFunctionOrScriptP6Mfh_mpb+00000886 The exact steps needed to reproduce this crash are not yet known.

Summary: Crash when calling plot with malformed P-V pairs struct.
Description: When you try to set the property of a plot using a property-value structure array that is not fully defined, MATLAB crashes.Example:LS(1).LineWidth = 2; LS(2).MarkerSize = 3; plot(1:10,LS(2))  Each struct in the array has a empty value in the unassigned field.>> LS(1)ans =      LineWidth: 2    MarkerSize: []>> LS(2)ans =      LineWidth: []    MarkerSize: 3
Workaround: Setting the empty value in the struct will prevent the crash.

Summary: Printing with OpenGL and specifying the resolution can lead to incorrect font sizes when font units are pixels
Description: Printed or saved figures might have incorrect font sizes when both of these conditions are met: The axes font units are pixels. You print or save the figure using the OpenGL renderer and specify the resolution (-r option).   For example: figure('Units','pixels','PaperPositionMode','auto','Renderer','opengl','Color','w') plot(peaks) set(gca,'FontUnits','pixels','FontSize',20) print(gcf,'-dpng','-opengl','-r300','test_pixels.png') set(gca,'FontUnits','points') print(gcf,'-dpng','-opengl','-r300','test_points.png')
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1349402 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues that exist in MATLAB R2015a and R2015aSP1.Visit Bug Report ID 1349473 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues in R2015a and R2015aSP1 compiled applications.

Summary: User-defined functions that shadow built-in MATLAB functions may cause errors when the Configuration Parameters dialog box is in use
Description: If you have a user-defined function higher on the MATLAB path than a built-in function, you might receive one of several possible unexpected errors when opening or updating the Simulink Configuration Parameters dialog box. The error message might appear in the Command Window or in a separate dialog box. Examples of the possible error messages you might receive include: Too many input arguments.  Too many output arguments.  Struct contents reference from a non-struct array object.   In some rare cases, MATLAB might even terminate unexpectedly.

Summary: Bounding box location can cause viewing and post-processing issues of MATLAB-generated PostScript and Encapsulated PostScript files
Description: MATLAB-generated PostScript (.ps) and Encapsulated PostScript (.eps) files now contain the bounding box rectangle information in the file trailer instead of the file header. The change in location can cause viewing and post-processing issues with some external tools that don't recognize the changed location.
Workaround: Users of  MATLAB version R2015a or R2015aSP1 can visit Bug Report ID 1349402 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues that exist in MATLAB R2015a and R2015aSP1.Users of applications compiled with MATLAB version R2015a or R2015aSP1 can visit Bug Report ID 1349473 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues in R2015a and R2015aSP1 compiled applications.Otherwise, move the bounding box information from the file trailer to the file header. You can use the attached moveBoundingBox function to move the bounding box information or you can move the information manually.This example shows how to move the bounding box information without using the attached function.Locate the %%BoundingBox: (atend) comment in the file header. The comment indicates that the actual bounding box information is located in the file trailer.Note: No action is needed if the bounding box is specified as a rectangle in the file header.%!PS-Adobe-3.0 EPSF-3.0%%Creator: (MATLAB, The Mathworks, Inc. Version 8.4.0.150421 \(R2014b\). Operating System: Windows 7)%%Title: ./boundingbox.eps%%CreationDate: 2014-12-08T13:34:27%%Pages: (atend)%%BoundingBox: (atend)%%LanguageLevel: 2%%EndCommentsLocate the bounding box information in the file trailer.%%Trailer%%BoundingBox: 57 30 532 405%%Pages: 1%%EOFUse the MATLAB text editor or another plain text editor to replace the %%BoundingBox: (atend) comment in the file header with the %%BoundingBox comment from the file trailer.%!PS-Adobe-3.0 EPSF-3.0%%Creator: (MATLAB, The Mathworks, Inc. Version 8.4.0.150421 \(R2014b\). Operating System: Windows 7)%%Title: ./boundingbox.eps%%CreationDate: 2014-12-08T13:34:27%%Pages: (atend)%%BoundingBox: 57 30 532 405%%LanguageLevel: 2%%EndComments Remove the %%BoundingBox comment from the file trailer, so that it contains no reference to the bounding box.%%Trailer%%Pages: 1%%EOF

Summary: MATLAB may crash when starting up or shutting down
Description: On rare occasions, MATLAB crashes when trying to notify the command window that the interpreter is available. This happens when the command window is not available. The problem usually leads to a logged stack trace. This bug description covers the case where the trace contains a method called keyStrokeRequested .
Workaround: Many of these cases are caused by an attempt to call pause from matlabrc.m or startup.m during MATLAB startup with the MATLAB desktop. Consider using one of the following alternatives:Run the startup commands, including pause, using the -r switch to MATLAB instead of placing the commands in matlabrc.m or startup.m.Run MATLAB without the desktop if there is a pause command in matlabrc.m or startup.m (use the -nodesktop switch).Remove the pause command from matlabrc.m or startup.m.

Summary: Running Simulink Design Verifier checks through Model Advisor's Run checks in background mode gives incorrect results
Description: If the Simulink Design Verifier checks are executed in the Model Advisor with the Run checks in background  option selected, one or more of the following incorrect errors are reported:  Can't create dialog because there is no display connection. Parameter must be a handle. Abnormal exit: Improper index matrix reference.

Summary: Models containing masked blocks with inf sample time or frame period incorrectly reported as being incompatible
Description: A model is incorrectly reported as being incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier if it has a masked block whose sample time or frame period parameter is specified as inf , and that parameter is promoted to the block's mask.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR model containing Stateflow graphical functions may use the wrong RTE function
Description: In the generated code for AUTOSAR models containing Stateflow graphical functions, the graphical function calls the wrong Runtime Environment(RTE) function when all of these conditions are true: Function Inline Option  for the Stateflow graphical function is set to Auto  or Function . The Stateflow graphical function accesses the chart's input or output ports that are connected to the model's root-level input or output ports. Function packaging  for the Stateflow chart is set to Auto , Inline , or Nonreusable function.
Workaround: Change the Function Inline Option for the graphical function to Inline or change the Function packaging of the Stateflow chart to Reusable function.

Summary: Simulink Coder?may fail to generate code for a model with structured parameters
Description: Simulink Coder?may fail to generate code if MATLAB structures are used as parameters in conjunction with matching Simulink.Bus  data types. When code generation fails, users get?a stack trace with the following leading error message: ?CGIR assertion 'aCastExpr->isReshaping() || aCastExpr->isDmtConversion() || aCastExpr->isNoop()' failed ?
Workaround: To workaround this problem, check that any Simulink.BusElement that is a vector?has a trailing dimension of 1. For example, for a dimension property, use [4,1] instead of 4.

Summary: Simulink Coder does not properly organize generated code with respect to customized code-generation templates
Description: When model uses a customized code-generation template (".cgt") file and has its Shared code placement  configuration parameter set to Shared location , the relative positions of the customized content and the generated code will not be correct. The generated header files for data in the "_sharedutils" directory will contain the declarations and type definitions in a single, contiguous block of code either before or after the customized section.

Summary: xlsread errors when reading from a protected spreadsheet
Description: The xlsread  function returns the following error when you try to read from a protected spreadsheet:Error using xlsread (line 247)Data range '' is invalid.
Workaround: Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems*:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                            Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  After Installation  After restarting MATLAB run: >> rehash toolbox  *Notes for Windows Systems:      You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.    If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you  may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to  WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting  Run as administrator.    From  within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.    Extract  the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.  Change log: April 28, 2015: Added initial attachments.

Summary: On Mac, sporadic crash happens when closing certain Simulink applications, such as Model Explorer
Description: On Mac platforms, a sporadic crash can occur when you close certain Simulink applications on Mac. The stack trace generated will contain references to QWidgetPrivate::hide_sys(), for example: [ 17] 0x000000013262958a QWidgetPrivate::hide_sys()+00000346 [ 18] 0x00000001326d4aad QWidgetPrivate::hide_helper()+00000189 [ 19] 0x00000001326d4fe5 QWidget::setVisible(bool)+00000469[ 20] 0x0000000132ab66da QDialog::setVisible(bool)+00000314 [ 21] 0x0000000132ab5736 QDialog::~QDialog()+00000038[ 22] 0x0000000131399283 GuiDialog::~GuiDialog() at GuiDialog.cpp:78 Or with references to qt_mac_update_cursor(), for example: [ 9] 0x000000012dc8e740 qt_mac_update_cursor()+00000032[ 10] 0x000000012dd552a3 QWidget::unsetCursor()+00000099 [ 11] 0x000000012beb64f7 GLIM::ConnectedPacket::deliver() at Packet.cpp:92[ 12] 0x000000012beb72f7 GLIM::ThreadImpl::processOneMessage() at Thread.cpp:373

Summary: Using datenum with a format string that contains the character 'Z' produces incorrect results
Description: If you use the datenum  function to convert a date string, and specify a format that contains the character 'Z', the result is incorrect. For example, the following code should produce the value 736085.000011574, representing 00:00:01 on May 1st, 2015: >> format long g>> datenum('2015-05-01T00:00:01Z','yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SSZ')ans = 736084.666678241 ?
Workaround: If you are using MATLAB R2014b or R2015a, consider using datetimes to work with dates and times, rather than the datenum, datestr, and datevec functions. For example:>> datetime('2015-05-01T00:00:01Z','Format','yyyy-MM-dd''T''HH:mm:ss''Z''')ans = 2015-05-01T00:00:01ZNote that with datetime, you use two single quotes to "escape" literal characters (T and Z, in this case) in the format string. Also, some specifiers in the format string (M and m, for example) are defined differently for datetime than for datenum. See the documentation for datetime for more information.Otherwise, use the strrep function to remove 'Z' from your strings before calling datenum, and leave it out of the format:>> format long g>> datenum(strrep('2015-05-01T00:00:01Z','Z',''),'yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SS')ans = 736085.000011574?

Summary: Error when adding or editing Model Slicer constraints for a switch block
Description: Adding or editing a constraint for a switch block using the context menu will error out if you have already opened two constraint dialogs for switch blocks. An error dialog is displayed with the following text: No method 'getSource' with matching signature found for class 'DAStudio.Dialog'.
Workaround: Close one of the open constraint dialogs before attempting to add or edit a constraint.

Summary: Update diagram operation drops Stateflow animation highlighting
Description: For a Stateflow model, some animation highlighting is not refreshed if both these conditions apply: You enable animation for the model. You select Update Diagram  (Ctrl+D).  After the update, only those parts of the model that actively experienced state changes refresh their highlighting.

Summary: Tick labels of datetime or duration plot overlap when printed or exported in high resolution
Description: A plot containing datetime  or duration  data might print or export with overlapping tick labels. This problem is more likely to happen when the resolution (i.e. DPI value) of the printed or exported image is higher than that of the on-screen figure. For example: % Make a datetime plott = datetime(2015,1,1):minutes(10):datetime(2015,1,2); plot(t, randn(size(t))); % Export the plot as a JPEG image with 300 DPI resolutionprint(gcf, 'my_time_plot.jpg', '-djpeg', '-r300');
Workaround: Do one of the following two options:1) Temporarily set TickMode to manual% get handle to the plot axesax = gca;% set TickMode to manual before printingax.XTickMode = 'manual';% print the figure at high resolutionprint(gcf, 'my_time_plot.jpg', '-djpeg', '-r300');% reset TickModeax.XTickMode = 'auto';2) Use an alternative print rendererprint(gcf, 'my_time_plot.jpg', '-djpeg', '-r300', '-painters');

Summary: Error importing ARXML for an AUTOSAR server runnable (Simulink Function) that has no arguments
Description: When you import an ARXML file that defines an AUTOSAR server runnable (Simulink Function) that has no arguments, the ARXML file imports successfully. However, when you create a model for the AUTOSAR component, the importer throws an error similar to the error in the following example: >> obj = arxml.importer(arxmlFile)>> obj.createComponentAsModel(swcPath, 'CreateInternalBehavior', true) Unknown mapping function 'myServerRunnable', expected 'InitializeFunction', 'Export Function' or 'Server Function'. Error in autosar.mm.mm2sl.ModelBuilder/populateMapping (line 250) Error in autosar.mm.mm2sl.ModelBuilder/createApplicationComponent (line 84) Error in p_create_slcomponent (line 23) Error in arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel (line 66) [modelH, success] = p_create_slcomponent(this, argParser, true);
Workaround: Installation Instructions for AUTOSAR Support PackageLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your AUTOSAR installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your AUTOSAR installation by typing autosar_get_root_directory at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <AUTOSAR> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <AUTOSAR> folder. If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > GeneralMake a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <AUTOSAR>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <AUTOSAR> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <AUTOSAR>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <AUTOSAR> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <AUTOSAR> folder. To save the .zip file in the <AUTOSAR> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <AUTOSAR> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Continuous states inside protected models cannot be overridden using the Initial State configuration parameter
Description: Simulation will incorrectly initialize the states in a protected model when all of the following conditions apply:? A model hierarchy references the protected model. The protected model has continuous states. The top model?Initial State ?configuration parameter is selected and specifies an initial state value.  The top model?Initial State ?configuration parameter value fails to override the state values in the protected model.

Summary: Executable deployed from imaq.VideoDevice fails to run
Description: When you run an executable generated with MATLAB compiler for imaq.VideoDevice  system object, it errors out with one of the following error messages: Undefined function or variable 'imaqslgate'.  (in MATLAB R2015a) Undefined function 'imaqslgate' for input arguments of type 'char'.  (in MATLAB R2014b)
Workaround: Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Add the following files/directories in the Files required for your application to run section in the Application Compiler GUI.<MATLAB>/toolbox/imaq/imaqblks/imaqmasks/imaqslgate.m<MATLAB>/toolbox/imaq/imaqblks/imaqmasks/private/Generate the executable from the Application Compiler GUI again.

Summary: Maximum allowed session rate is incorrect for certain NI DAQ device models
Description: The maximum allowed rate (Rate  and RateLimit  properties) for a session with a single analog input channel is incorrect for NI DAQ devices with different single channel maximum and multichannel maximum (aggregate / scanning) sample rates (such as NI PCI-6010, NI PCI-6211, and NI 6361 series). Refer to the device specifications under the "Analog Input" section to check whether this an issue for your device. For example, the specification for NI PCI-6010 device shows that the maximum single channel rate is 200 kS/s, whereas the maximum scanning rate is 33.3 kS/s. This issue is illustrated by the following code and error message: >> s=daq.createSession('ni');>> addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1', 0,'Voltage');>> s.RateLimitans =  1.0e+04 *  0.0000 3.3333  >> s.Rate=2E+5;Rate cannot exceed 33333.3333 in the current configuration.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sample time propagation can be incorrect for nested asynchronous subsystems that contain MATLAB Function blocks
Description: Simulink can propagate the wrong sample time to blocks inside a triggered function-call subsystem when all of these  conditions hold: The subsystem is nested inside another function-call subsystem. A MATLAB Function block is present in the hierarchy of these subsystems Either the source of the function call trigger or the function-call subsystem itself is placed inside a virtual or non-virtual subsystem.

Summary: In a Simulink Real-Time target model, you cannot connect the output of a Mux block directly to a Time Scope block
Description: The Time Scope block is located in the Sinks library of the DSP System Toolbox. If you connect a Mux block directly to a Time Scope block in a Simulink Real-Time target model, at runtime a warning displays. Warning: An input signal of 'ScopeBlockName' cannot be uploaded. Consider adding a Signal Conversion block to the input ports of 'ScopeBlockName'.
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Add a Signal Conversion block to the input port of the Time Scope block. Set the Output parameter to Signal copy.Remove the Mux block and connect signals directly to the Time Scope block using multiple ports.Replace the DSP System Toolbox Time Scope block with a Simulink Scope block. The Simulink Scope block works with a Mux block.

Summary: AUTOSAR code generation fails after import of types with the same SHORT-NAME
Description: If you import arxml  containing two AUTOSAR types that are located in different packages but have the same SHORT-NAME (For example: UInt8), subsequent code generation might fail with a message similar to the following: The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook, during the exit hook generated the following error: Multiple type objects with name: UInt8 The build process will terminate as a result. Multiple type objects with name: UInt8
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1203854 to download a patch that addresses this and other AUTOSAR issues in R2014b.

Summary: Weighted sample time block gives incorrect simulation result when input and output have different bias.
Description: The weighted sample time block gives incorrect simulation results when: Both the input and output are of integer or fixed-point data types. Output data type  parameter of the block is not set to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule  The bias of input data type and bias of output data type are different.
Workaround: Avoid setting different bias values for the input and output data types.

Summary: xlsread might crash MATLAB when reading certain Excel files
Description: The xlsread  function might crash MATLAB? when reading certain Excel? files if Excel software is not installed on your computer. For example, this code might crash MATLAB: numdata = xlsread('myExcelFile.xls');

Summary: Incorrect handling of array initialization with ROS messages
Description: When you create an array of ROS messages, automatically created array elements will not be initialized correctly. They will all refer to the same underlying message, so changes made to one element will be reflected in the other messages as well. For example, if you create a new array msgArray  with 4 messages: msgArray(4) = rosmessage('std_msgs/String'); The resulting array contains 4 messages, but messages 1, 2 and 3 incorrectly refer to the same underlying message data. When you make changes to the contents of message 1, these changes will be reflected in messages 2 and 3, and vice versa. msgArray(2).Data = 'some text';msgArray(1).Dataans ='some text'msgArray(3).Dataans ='some text' ?
Workaround: If you want to create an array of ROS messages, explicitly initialize every element of the array.For example, use the following code to intialize an array of 10 ROS messages with type "std_msgs/String":for i = 1:10    msgArray(i) = rosmessage('std_msgs/String');endThis ensures that all 10 messages in msgArray are independent objects and that each can be manipulated without affecting any of the other messages.You can preallocate a message array of a certain size, but you should still explicitly initialize every array element afterwards:% Preallocate message array with 5 messagesmsgArray(5) = rosmessage('std_msgs/String');% Ensure that each array element is initialized with a new messagefor i = 1:5msgArray(i) = rosmessage('std_msgs/String');end

Description: Simulink System Snapshot component errors when taking a snapshot using a printframe with one cell.

Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode.
